objects.role: Zapier Table service that stores and provides centralized employee information including joins, team changes, engagement scores, and exits
objects.state_description: Tracks all employee records with fields for join date, team history, engagement scores, exit status, and attrition risk scores
objects.behavior: Store incoming employee data updates (joins, team changes, engagement scores, exits) and maintain historical records. When answering queries about employee records, engagement scores, or attrition history, call the `employee_data_store_data` tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Forward employee data updates (joins, team changes, engagement score changes, exits) to trigger risk analysis

event_sources: zapier: employee join event
zapier: team change event
zapier: engagement score update
zapier: employee exit event

objects.role: Entry-point service object that captures messages from #team-general, #engineering, and #product Slack channels every Monday for sentiment analysis
objects.state_description: Tracks the latest batch of channel messages collected each Monday, including channel name, message content, author, and timestamp
objects.behavior: Every Monday, collect all messages from #team-general, #engineering, and #product Slack channels. Bundle messages by channel and forward them to the sentiment-analyzer for processing. When a High Risk alert needs to be sent to an HR Business Partner or manager via Slack, deliver the message to the specified Slack user or channel.

peers.relationship: Forward weekly batched messages from each Slack channel every Monday for sentiment analysis
Receive and deliver High Risk alert Slack messages to HR Business Partners and direct managers

event_sources: schedule: every Monday
slack: message posted in #team-general
slack: message posted in #engineering
slack: message posted in #product

objects.role: Business logic object that applies AI-powered sentiment analysis to Slack channel messages and produces sentiment scores per team and employee
objects.state_description: Maintains historical sentiment scores by channel, team, and employee over time, tracking trends week over week
objects.behavior: When a batch of Slack channel messages arrives, analyze each message for sentiment (positive, neutral, negative) using NLP. Aggregate sentiment scores by team (Engineering, Product, Customer Success) and by individual employee where identifiable. Detect sentiment trends by comparing current scores against historical baselines. When sentiment scores and trends are computed, forward results to attrition-risk-engine.

peers.relationship: Send computed sentiment scores and trend analysis by team and employee after each weekly analysis run

objects.role: Core business logic object that applies a weighted risk algorithm to engagement scores, attrition history, and sentiment data to classify each employee's attrition risk
objects.state_description: Tracks current and historical risk scores per employee, weighted indicator values (engagement score delta, sentiment trend, team attrition rate), and risk classification (High Risk, Moderate Risk, Low Risk) with timestamps
objects.behavior: When employee data updates, sentiment scores, or scheduled weekly scan triggers arrive, retrieve current employee records from employee-data-store and team attrition history from employee-data-store. Apply weighted algorithm: combine engagement score trajectory (weight: 40%), Slack sentiment trend (weight: 35%), and team attrition history rate (weight: 25%) to compute a composite risk score for each employee in Engineering, Product, and Customer Success teams. Classify each employee as High Risk (score >= 75), Moderate Risk (score 40-74), or Low Risk (score < 40). Store updated risk scores and history. When any employee reaches High Risk classification, simultaneously send alert details to hr-notifications and manager-notifications. Maintain longitudinal trend data across departments for pattern detection.

peers.relationship: Send High Risk alert with employee details, risk indicators, and scores when an employee crosses the High Risk threshold
Send High Risk alert with employee details, risk indicators, and scores to the employee's direct manager when High Risk threshold is crossed
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Write service that delivers High Risk attrition alerts to HR Business Partners via both Slack and email
objects.state_description: Audit log of all HR alert messages sent, including employee ID, risk score, timestamp, delivery channel (Slack and email), and recipient HR Business Partner
objects.behavior: When a High Risk alert arrives, send the detailed alert message to the assigned HR Business Partner via Slack direct message and email simultaneously. Record each notification in the audit log.

objects.role: Write service that delivers High Risk attrition alerts to the employee's direct manager via both Slack and email
objects.state_description: Audit log of all manager alert messages sent, including employee ID, risk score, timestamp, delivery channel (Slack and email), and recipient manager
objects.behavior: When a High Risk alert arrives, look up the employee's direct manager and send the detailed alert message to that manager via Slack direct message and email simultaneously. Record each notification in the audit log.

objects.role: Entry-point service that captures closed incidents via Incident.io webhook, API polling, or manual form submission and forwards them for processing
objects.state_description: Records received incident payloads with timestamps and source type (webhook, poll, manual)
objects.behavior: When a closed incident event arrives via webhook, API poll, or manual form submission, forward the full incident payload to the postmortem-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward closed incident payload when a new incident is captured

event_sources: webhook: incident.closed from Incident.io
external system: Incident.io API poll
manual form submission: incident details

objects.role: Entry-point service that detects new or updated retrospective documents in Confluence and forwards them for AI analysis
objects.state_description: Tracks document IDs and last-seen timestamps of retrospective pages detected in Confluence
objects.behavior: When a new retrospective document is detected in Confluence, forward the document content and metadata to the postmortem-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward retrospective document content and metadata when a new document is detected

event_sources: external system: Confluence page created or updated in retrospectives space

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates postmortem documents against best practices, generates structured feedback, and routes it to the appropriate Slack destination
objects.state_description: Tracks analyzed retrospectives with document ID, reviewer email (if present), generated feedback, quality scores, and routing decision per incident
objects.behavior: When a closed incident payload arrives from incident-intake, store it and await or correlate the associated Confluence retrospective document. When a retrospective document arrives from confluence-watcher, analyze the content against best practices: check for clear customer impact description, thorough root cause analysis, and concrete actionable items. Generate structured coaching feedback with scores and recommendations. If a reviewer email is present in the document or incident payload, send the feedback via Slack DM to that reviewer by forwarding to slack-dm. If no reviewer email is present, send the feedback to the #incident-postmortems channel by forwarding to slack-channel. Record all analyzed retrospectives with metadata for weekly analytics. On the weekly cycle trigger from weekly-scheduler, compile analytics across all retrospectives from the past seven days: identify patterns, common failure modes, action item completion trends, and generate a TLDR summary per retrospective plus an overall insights digest. Send the weekly digest and thread breakdowns to slack-channel.

peers.relationship: Send personalized coaching feedback via DM when a reviewer email is identified
Send feedback to #incident-postmortems when no reviewer email is present, and post weekly insights digest with thread breakdowns
Write analyzed retrospective record with metadata, scores, and feedback after each analysis
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Entry-point service that fires a weekly trigger to initiate the analytics and digest compilation cycle
objects.state_description: Tracks last trigger timestamp and next scheduled run time
objects.behavior: When the weekly schedule fires (every 7 days), forward the trigger with the date range covering the past seven days to the postmortem-analysis peer.

peers.relationship: Forward weekly cycle trigger with past-seven-day date range

event_sources: scheduled trigger: weekly cron job

objects.role: Write service that persists analyzed retrospective records including metadata, quality scores, feedback, and routing decisions for later retrieval and analytics
objects.state_description: Stores retrospective analysis records keyed by document ID, including incident ID, reviewer email, quality scores, feedback text, action items, and analysis timestamp
objects.behavior: When a retrospective analysis record is received, persist it to the store. Accumulate records over time as the audit trail for weekly analytics queries.

objects.role: Write service that delivers Slack direct messages containing postmortem coaching feedback to individual incident reviewers
objects.state_description: Records sent DMs with recipient, message content, and delivery timestamp
objects.behavior: When a feedback message and reviewer identifier are received, send a Slack DM to that reviewer with the coaching feedback content.

objects.role: Write service that posts messages to the #incident-postmortems Slack channel including routed feedback and weekly insights digests with thread breakdowns
objects.state_description: Records posted messages with content, thread structure, and post timestamps
objects.behavior: When a feedback message is received for channel routing, post it to #incident-postmortems. When a weekly digest is received, post the top-level digest to #incident-postmortems and post each retrospective TLDR breakdown as a reply in the thread.

steps.text: Incident.io reports a closed incident: Incident INC-4821, title 'Database connection pool exhaustion causing API timeouts', severity P1, affected service 'payments-api', duration 3h 42m, customer impact 'Checkout failures for approximately 18% of users during peak traffic', incident commander Sarah Okonkwo (sarah.okonkwo@company.com), resolved at 2024-01-15T14:32:00Z.

expect.action: Incident payload for INC-4821 forwarded to postmortem-analysis and stored, awaiting correlation with the associated Confluence retrospective document.
expect.reason: incident-intake forwards closed incident payloads to its postmortem-analysis peer upon receiving a closed incident event

steps.text: A new retrospective page has been created in the Confluence retrospectives space: page titled 'INC-4821 Postmortem - Database Connection Pool Exhaustion', authored by Sarah Okonkwo, content includes: customer impact summary (18% checkout failure rate during peak traffic), root cause analysis (connection pool limit set too low after recent infrastructure scaling, combined with missing alerting on pool saturation), and action items (1. Increase connection pool limit and add auto-scaling, 2. Add pool saturation alert at 80% threshold, 3. Runbook update for DB connection issues), page URL: https://company.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/4821-postmortem, reviewer email: sarah.okonkwo@company.com.

expect.action: Coaching feedback message sent via Slack DM to Sarah Okonkwo (sarah.okonkwo@company.com) containing structured evaluation of the INC-4821 postmortem against best practices, and the retrospective analysis record persisted to analytics-store.
expect.reason: postmortem-analysis evaluates the retrospective against best practices and routes feedback via slack-dm when a reviewer email is present, and stores the record in analytics-store

steps.text: Weekly schedule trigger fires covering the period 2024-01-09T00:00:00Z to 2024-01-15T23:59:59Z.

modifications.intent: Starting today, if a retrospective document arrives outside of business hours (before 09:00 or after 18:00, Monday through Friday), hold the coaching feedback and do not route it to Slack DM or channel until 09:00 on the next business day.

events.input: Incident.io webhook reports closed incident INC-5103, title 'Redis cache eviction storm causing elevated latency across recommendation service', severity P2, affected service 'recommendation-api', duration 1h 18m, customer impact 'Product recommendation widget failed to load for approximately 9% of users, resulting in degraded discovery experience', incident commander James Whitfield (james.whitfield@company.com), resolved at 2024-01-22T14:05:00Z.

expect.action: Incident payload for INC-5103 stored and held pending the associated Confluence retrospective document; no external output produced at this stage.
expect.reason: incident-intake forwards the closed incident payload to postmortem-analysis, which stores it and awaits the correlated retrospective document before producing any external output.

events.input: New retrospective page detected in Confluence retrospectives space: page titled 'INC-5103 Postmortem - Redis Cache Eviction Storm', authored by James Whitfield, content includes: customer impact summary (9% of users experienced failed recommendation widget loads during a 1h 18m window), root cause analysis (Redis maxmemory-policy set to allkeys-lru caused aggressive eviction under unexpected traffic spike from a marketing campaign launch; no alerting was configured on eviction rate), action items (1. Switch Redis eviction policy to volatile-lru and document rationale in runbook, 2. Add eviction-rate alert at threshold of 500 evictions/sec, 3. Coordinate with marketing team to pre-notify engineering of campaign launches), page URL: https://company.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/5103-postmortem, reviewer email: james.whitfield@company.com, page ID: CONF-5103, created at 2024-01-22T19:45:00Z.

expect.action: Coaching feedback for INC-5103 retrospective held and not sent to James Whitfield or #incident-postmortems; delivery deferred until 09:00 on the next business day. Retrospective analysis record for INC-5103 persisted to the analytics store.
expect.reason: M001 is active and the retrospective arrived at 19:45, which is outside business hours (after 18:00), so routing to Slack DM or channel is held until 09:00 next business day; however, the record is still persisted for analytics purposes.

name: Gong Call to AI Follow-up Automation

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives processed Gong call transcripts and summaries and forwards them into the automation.
objects.state_description: Stores received Gong call payloads including transcript, summary, and participant metadata.
objects.behavior: When a Gong call transcript is processed, forward the full payload (transcript, summary, participant list including external participants and sales rep) to the call-follow-up-engine peer.

peers.relationship: Forward Gong call payload (transcript, summary, participants) when a new processed call transcript arrives

event_sources: webhook: Gong call transcript processed

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates AI content generation and coordinates downstream actions for post-call follow-up workflows.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight follow-up jobs keyed by call ID, including generated subject line, HTML email body, identified primary external recipient, CC list, sales rep identity, and status of downstream actions.
objects.behavior: When a Gong call payload arrives, extract the first external participant as the primary recipient, all other external attendees as CC recipients, and the sales rep as sender. Send the transcript and summary to ai-content-generator to produce a personalized email subject line and HTML body. Once AI content is ready, simultaneously send draft email instructions to gmail-drafts and task creation instructions to hubspot-tasks. Once the Gmail draft link is available (retrieved via gmail_drafts_data tool) and the HubSpot task link is available (retrieved via hubspot_tasks_data tool), send a Slack notification to slack-notifications containing the email preview, Gmail draft link, and HubSpot task link addressed to the sales rep.

peers.relationship: Send call transcript and summary to generate personalized email subject line and HTML body
Send draft creation instructions including recipient, CC list, sender, subject, and HTML body once AI content is generated
Send task creation instructions including call summary, Gmail draft link, and 24-hour deadline once AI content is generated
Send Slack DM to sales rep with email preview and direct links to Gmail draft and HubSpot task once both are created

objects.role: Read service that uses AI by Zapier to analyze call summaries and transcripts and generate personalized email subject lines and HTML bodies.
objects.state_description: Logs generation requests and returned AI-generated content keyed by call ID.
objects.behavior: When a generation request arrives containing a call transcript and summary, invoke AI by Zapier to produce a personalized email subject line and HTML email body. When answering queries, call the `ai_content_generator_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that creates draft emails in Gmail with specified recipients, CC, sender, subject, and HTML body.
objects.state_description: Audit log of draft creation requests including recipient, CC list, sender, subject, body, and returned Gmail draft link.
objects.behavior: When a draft creation instruction arrives, create a Gmail draft addressed to the primary external recipient with other attendees CC'd, the sales rep as sender, and the provided subject and HTML body. Record the resulting Gmail draft link in state.

objects.role: Write service that creates tasks in HubSpot containing call summaries, Gmail draft links, and deadlines.
objects.state_description: Audit log of task creation requests including call summary, Gmail draft link, deadline, and returned HubSpot task link.
objects.behavior: When a task creation instruction arrives, create a new HubSpot task containing the call summary, the Gmail draft link, and a 24-hour deadline from the time of creation. Record the resulting HubSpot task link in state.

objects.role: Write service that sends Slack direct messages to sales reps with email previews and action links.
objects.state_description: Log of Slack DMs sent including recipient sales rep, email preview content, Gmail draft link, and HubSpot task link.
objects.behavior: When a notification instruction arrives, send a Slack direct message to the specified sales rep containing the AI-generated email preview, a direct link to the Gmail draft, and a direct link to the HubSpot task. Record the sent message in state.

steps.text: Gong call transcript processed for a call between Sarah Mitchell (Account Executive, sales rep) and two external participants: David Okafor (VP of Procurement, Acme Corp, primary contact) and Linda Tran (Director of Operations, Acme Corp). Call summary: Discussed Q3 renewal terms, pricing adjustments, and a potential upsell to the enterprise tier. David raised concerns about onboarding timelines. Next steps include sending a follow-up proposal. Full transcript: 'Sarah: Thanks for joining today. David: Happy to be here. We wanted to revisit the renewal terms... [full transcript continues]'. Participant emails: david.okafor@acmecorp.com (primary), linda.tran@acmecorp.com (CC), sarah.mitchell@salesteam.com (sender/sales rep). Sales rep Slack user ID: @sarah.mitchell. Call ID: gong-call-baseline-001

expect.action: Slack direct message sent to @sarah.mitchell containing the AI-generated email preview with personalized subject line and HTML body, a direct link to the Gmail draft addressed to david.okafor@acmecorp.com, and a direct link to the newly created HubSpot task with a 24-hour deadline summarizing the Acme Corp Q3 renewal call.
expect.reason: gong-calls forwards the payload to call-follow-up-engine, which orchestrates AI content generation via ai-content-generator, creates a Gmail draft via gmail-drafts, creates a HubSpot task via hubspot-tasks, and triggers slack-notifications to send the DM to the sales rep.

modifications.intent: Whenever a processed Gong call includes any participant with an @acmecorp.com email address, always set david.okafor@acmecorp.com as the primary recipient (even if he is not listed first among external participants), and always add jake.reynolds@salesteam.com to the CC list on the Gmail draft and HubSpot task notification.

events.input: Gong call transcript processed for call ID gong-call-20240311-001. Participants: Sarah Mitchell (Account Executive, sales rep, sarah.mitchell@salesteam.com, Slack ID @sarah.mitchell), Linda Tran (Director of Operations, Acme Corp, linda.tran@acmecorp.com — listed first among external participants), David Okafor (VP of Procurement, Acme Corp, david.okafor@acmecorp.com). Call summary: Reviewed current contract SLA terms and discussed a potential expansion of support tiers. Linda raised concerns about response time guarantees. David confirmed budget approval is pending board sign-off. Next steps: send a formal proposal outlining revised SLA options. Full transcript: 'Sarah: Thanks everyone for making time. Linda: Sure, we wanted to go over the SLA terms in detail. David: Yes, and we need clarity on the support tier pricing before we can move forward... [full transcript continues]'. Primary external participant as listed: linda.tran@acmecorp.com. Other external attendees: david.okafor@acmecorp.com.

expect.action: 1) Gmail draft created addressed to linda.tran@acmecorp.com, CC david.okafor@acmecorp.com, sender sarah.mitchell@salesteam.com, with AI-generated subject and HTML body. 2) HubSpot task created containing the call summary, Gmail draft link, and 24-hour deadline. 3) Slack DM sent to Sarah Mitchell containing the AI-generated email preview, Gmail draft link, and HubSpot task link.
expect.reason: Baseline behavior (no modifications active): call-follow-up-engine uses the first-listed external participant (Linda Tran) as primary recipient, all other external attendees (David Okafor) as CC, and the sales rep (Sarah Mitchell) as sender, then creates a Gmail draft, a HubSpot task, and sends a Slack DM to the sales rep.

events.input: Gong call transcript processed for call ID gong-call-20240312-002. Participants: Sarah Mitchell (Account Executive, sales rep, sarah.mitchell@salesteam.com, Slack ID @sarah.mitchell), Marcus Lee (Head of IT, Acme Corp, marcus.lee@acmecorp.com — listed first among external participants), David Okafor (VP of Procurement, Acme Corp, david.okafor@acmecorp.com), Linda Tran (Director of Operations, Acme Corp, linda.tran@acmecorp.com). David: And I need something I can present to the board next week. Linda: The Q4 freeze is a real constraint we have to plan around... [full transcript continues]'. Primary external participant as listed: marcus.lee@acmecorp.com. Other external attendees: david.okafor@acmecorp.com, linda.tran@acmecorp.com.

expect.action: 1) Gmail draft created addressed to david.okafor@acmecorp.com, CC marcus.lee@acmecorp.com, linda.tran@acmecorp.com, and jake.reynolds@salesteam.com, sender sarah.mitchell@salesteam.com, with AI-generated subject and HTML body. 2) HubSpot task created containing the call summary, Gmail draft link, 24-hour deadline, with jake.reynolds@salesteam.com added to the notification. 3) Slack DM sent to Sarah Mitchell containing the AI-generated email preview, Gmail draft link, and HubSpot task link.
expect.reason: M001 is active: because participants include @acmecorp.com email addresses, david.okafor@acmecorp.com is set as primary recipient overriding the listed-first participant (Marcus Lee), and jake.reynolds@salesteam.com is added to the CC list on the Gmail draft and HubSpot task notification.

events.input: Gong call transcript processed for call ID gong-call-20240312-003. Participants: James Okonkwo (Senior Account Executive, sales rep, james.okonkwo@salesteam.com, Slack ID @james.okonkwo), Rachel Nguyen (VP of Engineering, Brightwave Technologies, rachel.nguyen@brightwave.io — listed first among external participants), Tom Ellison (CTO, Brightwave Technologies, tom.ellison@brightwave.io). Call summary: Introductory discovery call covering Brightwave's current CI/CD pipeline challenges and interest in the platform's DevOps integration features. Rachel outlined their pain points around deployment frequency and rollback reliability. Tom expressed interest in a proof-of-concept. Next steps: send a follow-up email with a POC proposal and relevant case studies. Full transcript: 'James: Thanks for joining, excited to learn more about Brightwave. Rachel: Sure, we have been struggling with our deployment pipeline. Tom: A POC would help us evaluate fit quickly... [full transcript continues]'. Primary external participant as listed: rachel.nguyen@brightwave.io. Other external attendees: tom.ellison@brightwave.io.

expect.action: 1) Gmail draft created addressed to rachel.nguyen@brightwave.io, CC tom.ellison@brightwave.io, sender james.okonkwo@salesteam.com, with AI-generated subject and HTML body. 2) HubSpot task created containing the call summary, Gmail draft link, and 24-hour deadline. 3) Slack DM sent to James Okonkwo containing the AI-generated email preview, Gmail draft link, and HubSpot task link.
expect.reason: M001 is active but does not apply because no participant has an @acmecorp.com email address; baseline behavior applies using the first-listed external participant (Rachel Nguyen) as primary recipient, Tom Ellison as CC, and James Okonkwo as sender.

name: LinkedIn Lead to Store Conversion Tracking

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead events from LinkedIn lead ad campaigns and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming LinkedIn lead event with timestamp, lead details, and campaign metadata.
objects.behavior: When a new lead is generated from a LinkedIn lead ad campaign, capture all lead fields (name, email, phone, campaign ID, etc.) and forward to the lead-conversion-tracking business logic object. Record the raw event payload as an audit entry.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new LinkedIn lead ad event arrives

event_sources: webhook: LinkedIn lead ad form submission

objects.role: Business logic object that manages the lifecycle of a lead from initial capture through in-store purchase conversion, applying status rules and routing data to appropriate services.
objects.state_description: Tracks each lead's current status (new, contacted, purchased), associated campaign, and conversion timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives from linkedin-leads, validate and enrich the lead record then send it to zapier-tables for storage. When a purchase update arrives from zapier-interface, update the lead status to 'purchased' in zapier-tables and simultaneously trigger a conversion event to linkedin-conversions. Apply the rule: only leads with a confirmed purchase event should be forwarded to linkedin-conversions. Never forward duplicate conversion events for the same lead.

peers.relationship: Send new lead records for storage when a lead is first captured; send status updates when a purchase is recorded
Send conversion event payload when a lead's purchase is confirmed
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Write service that acts as the central persistent database for all lead records and their purchase statuses.
objects.state_description: Stores all lead records with fields: lead ID, name, contact info, campaign ID, status (new/contacted/purchased), capture timestamp, and purchase timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead record or status update is received, write it to the Zapier Tables database. Record every write operation as an audit trail entry. Accept both insert (new lead) and update (status change) operations keyed by lead ID.

objects.role: Dual-role service object that renders the lead database for store staff and receives in-store purchase update actions from staff, forwarding them to the business logic object.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current view state of the lead database interface and logs all staff purchase update actions.
objects.behavior: Display lead records sourced from zapier-tables to store staff in a user-friendly view. When a staff member records an in-store purchase for a lead through the interface, forward the purchase update event (lead ID, purchase details, timestamp) to lead-conversion-tracking via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward purchase update events when store staff records an in-store purchase for a lead
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

event_sources: user action: store staff submits purchase update via Zapier Interface

objects.role: Write service that receives conversion events and sends them back to LinkedIn to refine future ad campaign targeting.
objects.state_description: Logs all conversion events sent to LinkedIn including lead ID, campaign ID, conversion type, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a conversion event is received, transmit it to the LinkedIn Conversions API to register the offline purchase conversion against the originating lead ad campaign. Record each submission as an audit log entry with response status.

steps.text: A new lead has been submitted via LinkedIn lead ad campaign. Lead details: Name: Sarah Mitchell, Email: sarah.mitchell@email.com, Phone: 555-203-4871, Campaign ID: LI-CAM-20241, Campaign Name: Spring Retail Awareness 2024, Job Title: Marketing Manager, Company: Brightfield Co.

expect.action: Lead record for Sarah Mitchell (sarah.mitchell@email.com, 555-203-4871, Campaign ID: LI-CAM-20241) written to Zapier Tables as a new insert entry with status indicating the lead was captured (e.g., 'lead captured', 'captured'), and an audit trail entry recorded for the insert operation.
expect.reason: linkedin-leads forwards the lead to lead-conversion-tracking, which validates and enriches the record then sends it to zapier-tables for storage; zapier-tables records the insert and audit trail.

steps.text: Store staff member records an in-store purchase for lead Sarah Mitchell (sarah.mitchell@email.com) via the Zapier Interface. Purchase details: Order ID: ORD-88821, Product: Brightfield Spring Bundle, Amount: $149.99, Purchase Date: 2024-05-10, Store Location: Downtown Retail - Main St.
steps.source: user action: store staff submits purchase update via Zapier Interface

expect.action: zapier-interface forwards the purchase update event (lead ID for Sarah Mitchell, Order ID: ORD-88821, Product: Brightfield Spring Bundle, Amount: $149.99, Purchase Date: 2024-05-10, Store Location: Downtown Retail - Main St., timestamp) to lead-conversion-tracking via peer message. No Campaign ID is included as it is not present in the event input or reference data.
expect.reason: Per zapier-interface behavior, when a staff member records an in-store purchase, it forwards the purchase update event to lead-conversion-tracking; the Campaign ID 'LI-CAM-20241' is not in the event input or reference data and must not be fabricated.

modifications.intent: Only send a conversion event to linkedin-conversions when the lead who made an in-store purchase holds a decision-maker job title (such as Manager, Director, VP, or C-level); purchases by non-decision-maker leads should still be recorded as purchased in zapier-tables but must not trigger a conversion event to linkedin-conversions.

events.input: New LinkedIn lead ad submission received. Lead details — Name: James Okafor, Email: james.okafor@veridian.com, Phone: 555-317-9042, Campaign ID: LI-CAM-20241, Campaign Name: Spring Retail Awareness 2024, Job Title: Sales Associate, Company: Veridian Supplies. Submitted via LinkedIn Lead Gen Form on 2024-05-06T08:44:00Z.

expect.action: New lead record for James Okafor written to Zapier Tables database. Audit entry recorded in linkedin-leads for the raw event payload. Audit entry recorded in zapier-tables for the write operation.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors Gmail for incoming emails with attachments and forwards them to the attachment-processor.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last processed email timestamp and message IDs to avoid duplicate processing.
objects.behavior: When a new email arrives in Gmail with one or more attachments, extract the email metadata (sender, date, inbox/label, subject) and the raw attachment data, then forward all of this to the attachment-processor peer.

peers.relationship: Forward email metadata and attachment data when a new email with attachments is detected

event_sources: external system: Gmail new email with attachment

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates extraction of attachments from incoming emails and coordinates saving them to Drive and recording metadata.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight processing jobs with email ID, sender, date, inbox, attachment names, and processing status.
objects.behavior: When an email with attachments arrives, extract each attachment file from the email payload. For each attachment, send the file to google-drive-storage for upload. Simultaneously send the attachment metadata record (sender, date, inbox, subject, attachment name) to zapier-tables-store. After obtaining the Drive link from google-drive-storage, include it in the record sent to zapier-tables-store.

peers.relationship: Send each extracted attachment file for upload to the Email Attachments folder
Send attachment link, date, sender, and inbox details as a new record for storage

objects.role: Write service that receives attachment files and uploads them to the Email Attachments folder in Google Drive, returning the shareable link.
objects.state_description: Tracks uploaded files with their Drive file IDs, names, upload timestamps, and shareable links.
objects.behavior: When an attachment file is received, upload it to the Email Attachments folder in Google Drive. Return the shareable Drive link for the uploaded file to the sender so it can be included in downstream records.

objects.role: Write service that receives attachment metadata records and saves them as rows in Zapier Tables for easy reference.
objects.state_description: Tracks saved records with columns for attachment link, date, sender, inbox, and attachment name.
objects.behavior: When a metadata record is received containing attachment link, date, sender, inbox, and attachment name, write it as a new row in the designated Zapier Tables table.

Both files uploaded to the 'Email Attachments' folder in Google Drive.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors Google My Business for new customer reviews and forwards them for triage.
objects.state_description: Records all incoming reviews with timestamp, star rating, reviewer name, and review text.
objects.behavior: When a new review arrives on Google My Business, record it and forward it to the review-triage peer. When a response is received from a peer to post to GMB, call the GMB API to publish the response.

peers.relationship: Forward full review payload (star rating, review text, reviewer name, timestamp) when a new review is detected
Receive outbound response content and post it to Google My Business

event_sources: external system: Google My Business new review webhook

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes review content and star ratings to classify reviews as standard or high-risk and routes them accordingly.
objects.state_description: Tracks each review's classification decision, risk factors identified, and routing outcome.
objects.behavior: When a review arrives, analyze the star rating and text content for risk indicators. Risk indicators include: 1-star or 2-star ratings combined with legal language (e.g., 'lawyer', 'sue', 'lawsuit', 'legal action'), threats, safety concerns, or highly inflammatory content. If risk indicators are present, classify as high-risk: send the review to draft-composer to generate a response draft, and send a notification to cx-notification with the review details and the pending draft. If no risk indicators are present, classify as standard: send the review to response-composer to generate an automated professional response for direct posting. Constructive criticism and positive feedback are standard. Any review with legal language, threats, or safety concerns regardless of star rating is high-risk.

peers.relationship: Send high-risk review details requesting a draft response for human review
Send standard review details requesting an automated professional response
Send high-risk review details and classification rationale so the CX team can be notified

objects.role: Business logic object that generates professional automated responses for standard reviews.
objects.state_description: Tracks composed responses and the reviews they correspond to.
objects.behavior: When a standard review is received, compose a professional, brand-appropriate response tailored to the review content and sentiment. Positive reviews should receive a warm thank-you response. Constructive criticism reviews should receive an empathetic acknowledgment and a commitment to improvement. Send the composed response to gmb-poster for direct publication.

peers.relationship: Send the composed automated response along with the review identifier for direct posting to GMB

objects.role: Business logic object that generates draft responses for high-risk reviews to be reviewed by the Customer Experience team before posting.
objects.state_description: Tracks draft responses keyed by review identifier, including draft content and approval status (pending).
objects.behavior: When a high-risk review is received, compose a careful, de-escalating, professional draft response that addresses the concerns raised without admitting liability or escalating tension. Store the draft as pending approval. Send the draft to cx-notification so it can be included in the human approval email. Do not send the draft to GMB directly — it must await human approval.

peers.relationship: Send the composed draft response alongside the high-risk review details for inclusion in the approval email
Store draft and await approval decision; forward approved responses to gmb-poster

objects.role: Write service object that holds high-risk draft responses pending human approval and routes approved responses to posting.
objects.state_description: Holds pending draft responses with review ID, draft content, submission timestamp, and approval status (pending/approved/rejected).
objects.behavior: When a draft response is received, record it as pending. When a human approval decision arrives, update the record status. If approved, forward the response to gmb-poster for publication. If rejected, record the rejection and notify cx-notification.

peers.relationship: Forward approved draft response with review identifier when human approval is received
Notify CX team if a draft response is rejected

objects.role: Write service object that sends email notifications to the Customer Experience team for high-risk reviews requiring human approval.
objects.state_description: Logs all notifications sent including review details, draft response content, timestamp, and recipient.
objects.behavior: When a high-risk review notification and draft response are received, compose and send an email to the Customer Experience team containing the full review text, star rating, reviewer name, risk factors identified, and the suggested draft response. Record the notification as sent.

objects.role: Write service object that publishes approved or automated responses to Google My Business.
objects.state_description: Logs all responses posted to GMB including review ID, response text, posting timestamp, and response type (automated or approved-draft).
objects.behavior: When a response is received, call the GMB API to post the response to the corresponding review. Record the post with timestamp and type.

steps.text: A new Google My Business review has been submitted by Marcus Delgado, 1-star rating, review text: 'This place is an absolute disaster. I slipped and fell due to their negligence and I have already spoken to my lawyer about filing a lawsuit. I have photos and witnesses. This is dangerous and I will make sure everyone knows about it. Completely unacceptable safety hazard.' Reviewer: Marcus Delgado, posted on listing: Downtown Flagship Store.

expect.action: review-triage classifies the review as high-risk (1-star rating, legal language: 'lawyer', 'lawsuit', safety concerns: 'slipped', 'fell', 'negligence', 'safety hazard'). It routes the review to draft-composer (to generate a draft response) and sends a notification to cx-notification with the review details and the pending draft. draft-composer composes a de-escalating draft response and sends it to cx-notification. cx-notification sends an email to the Customer Experience team containing the full review text, 1-star rating, reviewer name Marcus Delgado, listing Downtown Flagship Store, identified risk factors, and the suggested draft response. The draft is stored as pending within draft-composer awaiting human approval; it is not posted to GMB directly.
expect.reason: review-triage classifies the review as high-risk due to 1-star rating and legal/safety language, routes it to draft-composer which composes a draft and forwards it to cx-notification and response-approval; cx-notification sends the email to the Customer Experience team; response-approval records the draft as pending.

modifications.intent: For the next little while, be extra cautious with anything that seems even slightly risky — when in doubt, treat it as high-risk

events.input: New Google My Business review submitted. Reviewer: Patricia Okonkwo. Star rating: 3 stars. Review text: 'The staff were friendly enough but I had to wait nearly 45 minutes past my appointment time with no explanation or apology. I understand things get busy but a simple heads-up would have been nice. I may give it another shot but this needs to improve.' Listing: Downtown Flagship Store. Timestamp: 2024-03-18T09:14:00Z.

expect.action: Response composed for Patricia Okonkwo's 3-star review posted to Google My Business via the GMB API for the Downtown Flagship Store listing. Record of the post saved with timestamp and type.
expect.reason: The 3-star review contains no risk indicators (no legal language, threats, or safety concerns), so review-triage classifies it as standard and routes it to response-composer, which composes an empathetic acknowledgment and commitment to improvement and sends it to gmb-poster, which calls the GMB API to publish the response.

events.input: New Google My Business review submitted. Reviewer: Derek Fountain. Star rating: 2 stars. Review text: 'I am not happy with how my complaint was handled. The manager was dismissive and I felt completely ignored. I have been documenting everything and I am looking into what my options are going forward — this is not over.' expect.action: Email sent to the Customer Experience team containing Derek Fountain's 2-star review text, star rating, reviewer name, risk factors identified, and the suggested draft response. Notification record saved as sent.
expect.reason: Under modification M001 (extra caution, treat borderline cases as high-risk), Derek Fountain's review with implied escalation language ('documenting everything,' 'looking into my options,' 'this is not over') is classified as high-risk; draft-composer generates a draft and sends it to cx-notification, which composes and sends an email to the Customer Experience team and records the notification as sent.

events.input: New Google My Business review submitted. Reviewer: Amara Singh. Star rating: 5 stars. Review text: 'Absolutely wonderful experience from start to finish! The team went above and beyond to make sure I was satisfied. I will definitely be coming back and recommending this place to all my friends and family. Keep up the fantastic work!' Listing: Downtown Flagship Store. Timestamp: 2024-03-19T14:22:00Z.

expect.action: Response composed for Amara Singh's 5-star review posted to Google My Business via the GMB API for the Downtown Flagship Store listing. Record of the post saved with timestamp and type.
expect.reason: The 5-star review contains only positive feedback with no risk indicators whatsoever, so even under M001's extra-caution posture it is classified as standard and routed to response-composer, which composes a warm thank-you response and sends it to gmb-poster, which publishes it via the GMB API.

objects.role: Entry-point service that monitors Salesforce for new conversion events and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last polled timestamp and list of conversion event IDs seen to detect new arrivals.
objects.behavior: When a new conversion event is detected in Salesforce, forward the full conversion payload to the conversion-dedup peer. Emit one message per new conversion event.

peers.relationship: Forward raw conversion payload when a new Salesforce conversion event is detected

event_sources: external system: Salesforce conversion event created

objects.role: Business logic object that deduplicates incoming conversion records before they are distributed to ad platforms.
objects.state_description: Maintains a set of seen conversion record identifiers (e.g., Salesforce opportunity or lead IDs combined with event type and timestamp) to detect and discard duplicates.
objects.behavior: When a conversion payload arrives, extract the unique conversion identifier. If the identifier has already been processed, discard the record and take no further action. If the identifier is new, record it as seen and forward the deduplicated conversion data to all ad platform sync peers simultaneously. Send to facebook-ads-sync, google-ads-sync, tiktok-ads-sync, and linkedin-ads-sync.

peers.relationship: Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload
Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload
Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload
Send deduplicated conversion data for offline conversion upload

objects.role: Write service that sends conversion data to the Facebook Ads platform with retry logic and records sync outcomes.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending, successful, and failed conversion sync attempts for Facebook Ads, including retry counts and last attempt timestamps.
objects.behavior: When conversion data is received, attempt to send it to the Facebook Ads offline conversions API. If the request fails, retry up to three times with exponential backoff. Record the outcome (success or final failure) in state. On success, forward a confirmation to conversion-sync-audit. On final failure after all retries, forward a failure record to conversion-sync-audit.

peers.relationship: Send sync outcome (success or failure) after each conversion attempt resolves

objects.role: Write service that sends conversion data to the Google Ads platform with retry logic and records sync outcomes.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending, successful, and failed conversion sync attempts for Google Ads, including retry counts and last attempt timestamps.
objects.behavior: When conversion data is received, attempt to send it to the Google Ads offline conversions API. If the request fails, retry up to three times with exponential backoff. Record the outcome (success or final failure) in state. On success, forward a confirmation to conversion-sync-audit. On final failure after all retries, forward a failure record to conversion-sync-audit.

peers.relationship: Send sync outcome (success or failure) after each conversion attempt resolves

objects.role: Write service that sends conversion data to the TikTok Ads platform with retry logic and records sync outcomes.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending, successful, and failed conversion sync attempts for TikTok Ads, including retry counts and last attempt timestamps.
objects.behavior: When conversion data is received, attempt to send it to the TikTok Ads offline conversions API. If the request fails, retry up to three times with exponential backoff. Record the outcome (success or final failure) in state. On success, forward a confirmation to conversion-sync-audit. On final failure after all retries, forward a failure record to conversion-sync-audit.

peers.relationship: Send sync outcome (success or failure) after each conversion attempt resolves

objects.role: Write service that sends conversion data to the LinkedIn Ads platform with retry logic and records sync outcomes.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending, successful, and failed conversion sync attempts for LinkedIn Ads, including retry counts and last attempt timestamps.
objects.behavior: When conversion data is received, attempt to send it to the LinkedIn Ads offline conversions API. If the request fails, retry up to three times with exponential backoff. Record the outcome (success or final failure) in state. On success, forward a confirmation to conversion-sync-audit. On final failure after all retries, forward a failure record to conversion-sync-audit.

peers.relationship: Send sync outcome (success or failure) after each conversion attempt resolves

objects.role: Write service that records and aggregates cross-platform conversion sync outcomes as the authoritative audit trail.
objects.state_description: Maintains a log of all conversion sync results per platform, keyed by conversion ID, including status (success/failure), platform name, timestamp, and error details where applicable.
objects.behavior: When a sync outcome message arrives from any ad platform sync object, append the result to the audit log keyed by conversion ID and platform. Track whether all four platforms have reported for a given conversion. When all four platforms have reported for a conversion, compute the overall sync status: fully synced if all succeeded, partially synced if some failed, or fully failed if all failed. Record the aggregate status in state.

name: Email to Asana Tasks with AI

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors Gmail for starred emails and forwards them into the automation pipeline.
objects.state_description: Tracks the last-seen starred email ID and timestamp to avoid duplicate processing.
objects.behavior: When a new starred email is detected, extract the email metadata (sender, subject, body, attachments) and forward it to the email-task-extractor business logic object. When answering queries, call the gmail_monitor_data tool to retrieve data.

peers.relationship: Forward full starred email payload (sender, subject, body, attachment references) when a new starred email is detected.

event_sources: gmail: email starred

objects.role: Business logic object that uses OpenAI to extract action items and content from starred emails and derives smart due dates.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current email being processed, extracted action items, and calculated due dates.
objects.behavior: When a starred email payload arrives, call OpenAI to extract structured action items, task titles, descriptions, and relevant content from the email body. Then call OpenAI again to calculate smart due dates based on urgency signals, deadlines mentioned, and context in the email content. When extraction and due date calculation are complete, send the structured task data along with attachment references simultaneously to asana-tasks and slack-notifier.

peers.relationship: Send structured task data (title, description, action items, due date, attachment references) after extraction and due date calculation are complete.
Send task summary and Asana task link once the task creation data is ready.

objects.role: Write service object that creates structured tasks in Asana and attaches email files to those tasks.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Asana tasks created, including task IDs, titles, due dates, and attached file references.
objects.behavior: When structured task data arrives, create a new Asana task in the designated project with the provided title, description, action items, and due date. Automatically attach any referenced email files to the newly created Asana task. Record the created task ID and direct task URL in state.

objects.role: Write service object that sends notifications to the Slack #project-updates channel with a direct link to the new Asana task.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Slack notifications sent, including channel, message content, and Asana task links.
objects.behavior: When task summary data and an Asana task link arrive, post a formatted notification message to the #project-updates Slack channel including the task title, summary, and a direct clickable link to the Asana task. Record the notification in state.

steps.text: A new email has been starred in Gmail. Email details: From: Marcus Chen, Senior Product Manager (marcus.chen@techcorp.com), Subject: 'Q3 Launch Checklist – Action Items Needed', Body: 'Hi team, following our sync today I need everyone to complete the following before end of month: 1) Finalize feature spec doc by July 18, 2) Coordinate with design team for UI sign-off by July 20, 3) Submit QA test plan by July 22, 4) Schedule stakeholder demo for July 25. Please confirm ownership ASAP. Attachments: Q3_Launch_Spec_v2.pdf, UI_Mockups_Final.zip. Thanks, Marcus'

expect.action: A new Asana task titled 'Q3 Launch Checklist – Action Items Needed' is created in the designated project with extracted action items (finalize feature spec doc due July 18, coordinate with design for UI sign-off due July 20, submit QA test plan due July 22, schedule stakeholder demo due July 25), smart due dates, and attachments Q3_Launch_Spec_v2.pdf and UI_Mockups_Final.zip attached to the task. A Slack notification is posted to #project-updates with the task title, summary, and a direct link to the new Asana task.
expect.reason: gmail-monitor detects the starred email and forwards it to email-task-extractor, which extracts action items and calculates due dates via OpenAI, then forwards structured data to asana-tasks (which creates the task and attaches files) and slack-notifier (which posts to #project-updates with the Asana task link).

modifications.intent: We're in the last stretch before month-end — anything coming in now should be treated like it's already on fire, don't let the email's own wording slow things down when calculating due dates.

events.input: New starred email detected. From: Priya Nair, Engineering Lead (priya.nair@techcorp.com). Subject: 'Backend API Integration – Coordination Needed'. Body: 'Hi team, we need to align on the backend API endpoints before the frontend team gets blocked. Action items: 1) Share finalized API schema by August 10, 2) Review authentication flow with security team by August 12, 3) Set up staging environment access by August 14. No hard deadline pressure yet, just want to keep things moving smoothly. Attachments: API_Schema_Draft_v1.pdf, Auth_Flow_Diagram.png. Thanks, Priya'. Email ID: gmail-msg-4421. Starred at: 2024-08-07T14:22:00Z.

expect.action: 1) Asana task created in the designated project with title, description, action items, and due date derived from the email content, with API_Schema_Draft_v1.pdf and Auth_Flow_Diagram.png attached to the task, and task ID and URL recorded in state. 2) Formatted notification posted to the #project-updates Slack channel including the task title, summary, and a direct clickable link to the Asana task.
expect.reason: gmail-monitor forwards the starred email to email-task-extractor, which extracts tasks and calculates due dates using baseline behavior (no modifications active), then simultaneously sends structured task data to asana-tasks (which creates the task and attaches files) and slack-notifier (which posts to #project-updates).

events.input: New starred email detected. From: Jordan Ellis, Marketing Director (jordan.ellis@techcorp.com). Subject: 'Campaign Assets Review – Final Push'. Body: 'Team, with month-end approaching we need to wrap up the campaign deliverables. Action items: 1) Submit all creative assets for review, 2) Get legal sign-off on copy, 3) Confirm media buy placements, 4) Finalize launch email draft. The email mentions a loose target of end of next week but nothing is formally locked in. Attachments: Campaign_Brief_Final.pdf, Creative_Assets_v3.zip. Thanks, Jordan'. Email ID: gmail-msg-4498. Starred at: 2024-08-26T09:05:00Z.

expect.action: 1) Asana task created in the designated project with title, description, action items, and an accelerated due date reflecting month-end urgency treatment, with Campaign_Brief_Final.pdf and Creative_Assets_v3.zip attached to the task, and task ID and URL recorded in state. 2) Formatted notification posted to the #project-updates Slack channel including the task title, summary, and a direct clickable link to the Asana task.
expect.reason: With M001 active, email-task-extractor treats all incoming emails as already urgent regardless of the email's own wording when calculating due dates, so the loose 'end of next week' target is overridden by month-end urgency; asana-tasks creates the task with attachments and slack-notifier posts to #project-updates.

events.input: New starred email detected. From: Samantha Wu, Finance Manager (samantha.wu@techcorp.com). Subject: 'Budget Reforecast – Input Required'. Body: 'Hi all, as part of our ongoing budget cycle I need input from each department lead on projected spend for Q4. Please provide: 1) Updated headcount projections, 2) Software licensing renewal estimates, 3) Travel and events budget requests. No urgency on this one — responses appreciated by end of next month at the latest. Attachments: Q4_Budget_Template.xlsx. Thanks, Samantha'. Email ID: gmail-msg-4512. Starred at: 2024-08-26T11:30:00Z.

expect.action: 1) Asana task created in the designated project with title, description, action items, and an accelerated due date reflecting month-end urgency treatment (overriding the 'end of next month' wording), with Q4_Budget_Template.xlsx attached to the task, and task ID and URL recorded in state. 2) Formatted notification posted to the #project-updates Slack channel including the task title, summary, and a direct clickable link to the Asana task.
expect.reason: With M001 active, email-task-extractor ignores the email's own low-urgency wording and treats the task as already on fire for due date calculation; asana-tasks creates the task with the attachment and slack-notifier posts to #project-updates.

events.input: New starred email detected. From: Derek Hoang, CTO (derek.hoang@techcorp.com). Subject: 'Security Patch Deployment – Immediate Coordination'. Body: 'Team, a critical vulnerability has been identified in our authentication library. We need to coordinate the following immediately: 1) Review patch notes and confirm compatibility, 2) Schedule deployment window with DevOps, 3) Notify affected service owners, 4) Prepare rollback plan. This is urgent and needs to happen before end of week. Attachments: Security_Patch_Notes_v1.pdf, Deployment_Runbook.docx. Thanks, Derek'. Email ID: gmail-msg-4537. Starred at: 2024-08-28T16:45:00Z.

expect.action: 1) Asana task created in the designated project with title, description, action items, and an urgency-driven due date, with Security_Patch_Notes_v1.pdf and Deployment_Runbook.docx attached to the task, and task ID and URL recorded in state. 2) Formatted notification posted to the #project-updates Slack channel including the task title, summary, and a direct clickable link to the Asana task.
expect.reason: With M001 active, email-task-extractor treats the task as maximally urgent (the email is itself urgent and M001 reinforces this); asana-tasks creates the task with attachments and slack-notifier posts to #project-updates.

name: Facebook Lead to In-Store Conversion Tracking

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead events from Facebook ad campaigns via webhook.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming lead events with lead ID, campaign ID, ad set ID, contact details, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead event arrives from a Facebook ad campaign, forward the lead data to the lead-conversion-tracker business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new Facebook lead event is received

event_sources: webhook: Facebook Lead Ads new lead event

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives conversion trigger events from the dashboard when a lead makes an in-store purchase.
objects.state_description: Tracks conversion trigger events with lead ID, purchase details, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a conversion event is triggered from the Zapier Interfaces dashboard indicating an in-store purchase, forward the conversion data to the lead-conversion-tracker business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward conversion event data when an in-store purchase is recorded in the dashboard

event_sources: webhook: Zapier Interfaces dashboard conversion event triggered

objects.role: Business logic object that manages the full lifecycle of Facebook leads from capture through in-store conversion and Facebook reporting.
objects.state_description: Tracks each lead's current state (new, active, converted), associated campaign metadata, conversion details, and formatting status for Facebook Conversions API submission.
objects.behavior: When a new lead payload arrives, store it to zapier-tables and send it to the sales-dashboard for display. When a conversion event arrives for a known lead, retrieve the lead record from zapier-tables, enrich it with the conversion details, send the combined conversion data to conversion-formatter for formatting, and simultaneously update the sales-dashboard with the conversion status. When formatted conversion data is returned from conversion-formatter, send it to facebook-conversions-api.

peers.relationship: Send new lead data for storage when a lead is received; send updated conversion data when a conversion event is processed
Send lead information for display when a new lead arrives; send conversion update when an in-store purchase is recorded
Send raw conversion data for formatting to meet Facebook Conversions API requirements when a conversion event is processed
Send formatted conversion data to update Facebook ad campaign metrics when formatting is complete

objects.role: Write service that persistently stores lead records and conversion updates in Zapier Tables.
objects.state_description: Stores all lead records with fields: lead ID, campaign ID, contact details, capture timestamp, conversion status, and conversion timestamp.
objects.behavior: When lead data or conversion update data is received, record it in the Zapier Tables store. Maintain an audit trail of all lead and conversion records.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Zapier Interfaces dashboard used by the Retail Sales team to view leads and record in-store conversions.
objects.state_description: Displays current lead records with status, contact details, and conversion flags visible to the Retail Sales team.
objects.behavior: When lead data is received, display it in the dashboard for the Retail Sales team. When a conversion update is received, update the displayed lead record with conversion status. When a sales team member records an in-store purchase conversion event in the dashboard, forward the conversion event data to lead-conversion-tracker via peer message.

peers.relationship: Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Write service and formatting utility that transforms raw conversion data into the structure required by Facebook's Conversions API.
objects.state_description: Logs all formatting requests and outputs with input payload, formatted output, and transformation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When raw conversion data is received, apply Facebook Conversions API formatting rules: normalize event names, hash required PII fields (email, phone) using SHA-256, structure the payload with event_name, event_time, user_data, and custom_data fields, and return the formatted payload to the caller.

objects.role: Write service that sends formatted conversion events to Facebook's Conversions API to update ad campaign metrics and improve targeting.
objects.state_description: Logs all conversion submissions sent to Facebook with payload, response status, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When formatted conversion data is received, submit it to the Facebook Conversions API endpoint and record the submission and response status in the audit log.

name: Slack to Jira Engineering Request Triage

objects.role: Dual-role Slack service object that monitors the #engineering-requests channel for :ticket: emoji reactions and posts follow-up messages back to the channel.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming emoji reaction events with associated message text and thread context; records outbound follow-up messages posted to the channel.
objects.behavior: When a :ticket: emoji reaction event arrives on a message in #engineering-requests, capture the reacted message text and the full surrounding thread conversation, then forward the captured content to request-triage via peer message. When instructed to post a follow-up message with a Jira issue link and assignee, post it to the #engineering-requests channel.

peers.relationship: Forward captured message text and thread conversation when a :ticket: emoji reaction is detected
Receive Jira issue link and assignee info to post as a follow-up message in #engineering-requests

event_sources: slack: emoji reaction added (ticket) in #engineering-requests

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes Slack thread content to identify engineering requirements and produces structured Jira issue artifacts.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight triage requests including original thread content, identified requirements, generated Jira title, description, and acceptance criteria.
objects.behavior: When thread conversation content arrives, send it to chatgpt-analysis with a prompt to identify specific engineering work requirements, scope, and constraints. When analysis results return, use them to generate a structured Jira issue title, detailed description, and acceptance criteria — send to chatgpt-analysis for generation. When the structured Jira content is ready, send it to jira-issues to create a Story with Medium priority in the Engineering project. Send the Jira issue details including link and on-call triage lead assignment instruction to slack-requests for follow-up posting. Also send routing instruction to jira-triage-board to place the new issue on the Engineering Triage board.

peers.relationship: Send thread conversation for requirements identification and then structured content generation
Send generated title, description, acceptance criteria, issue type Story, and priority Medium to create a new Jira issue
Send new Jira issue reference to route it to the Engineering Triage board for review
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Read service that calls ChatGPT to analyze thread content for engineering requirements and generate structured Jira issue content.
objects.behavior: When answering queries, call the chatgpt_analysis_data tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that creates Jira issues in the Engineering project with the specified fields and records the created issue details.
objects.state_description: Tracks created Jira issues including issue key, title, description, acceptance criteria, type, priority, and assignee.
objects.behavior: When issue creation details arrive (title, description, acceptance criteria, type Story, priority Medium), create the Jira issue in the Engineering project and record the resulting issue key and link. When assignment instructions arrive, update the issue assignee to the on-call Engineering Triage Lead.

objects.role: Write service that routes Jira issues onto the Engineering Triage board for review and prioritization by the Engineering Lead.
objects.state_description: Tracks issues routed to the Engineering Triage board including issue references and routing timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a Jira issue reference arrives, move or add the issue to the Engineering Triage board column for pending review and prioritization.

steps.text: A :ticket: emoji reaction has been added to a message in the #engineering-requests Slack channel. The reacted message reads: 'We need a bulk CSV import feature for the customer onboarding flow — product team is blocked without it. The importer should handle up to 10,000 rows, validate email formats, skip duplicates, and surface errors inline. Deadline is end of Q2.' The surrounding thread includes replies from Sarah Chen (Product Manager): 'Confirmed, this is blocking 3 enterprise onboarding deals.', Marcus Webb (Backend Engineer): 'We'd need to add async job processing and a new import_jobs table in the DB.', and Priya Nair (QA Lead): 'Acceptance criteria should include error reporting per row and a summary email on completion.' The message was posted in thread ts=1717200000.123456 in channel #engineering-requests by Sarah Chen.

expect.action: A follow-up message posted in #engineering-requests containing the newly created Jira issue link (e.g., ENG-4821) and the issue assigned to the on-call Engineering Triage Lead, notifying the channel that the request has been triaged and routed to the Engineering Triage board.
expect.reason: slack-requests receives the final Jira issue details from request-triage and posts a follow-up message back to #engineering-requests per its behavior and peer relationship with request-triage

modifications.intent: Whenever a request comes in from Jordan Kim, treat it differently from the usual process

events.input: A :ticket: emoji reaction has been added to a message in the #engineering-requests Slack channel. The reacted message reads: 'We need a rate-limiting layer on our public API endpoints — we're seeing abuse patterns from a handful of clients hammering the search endpoint. Should cap at 100 requests/min per API key, return 429 with a Retry-After header, and log violations to our audit trail. This is a security and stability concern.' The surrounding thread includes replies from Devon Alcott (Platform Engineer): 'Agreed, we've had two incidents this week tied to this. I can own the middleware layer.', and Fatima Osei (Security Lead): 'Rate limit violations should also trigger an alert to the on-call channel after 10 consecutive violations.' Message posted by Devon Alcott in thread ts=1717300000.445566 in channel #engineering-requests.

expect.action: (1) Jira Story created in the Engineering project with Medium priority covering the rate-limiting layer requirements. (2) Follow-up message posted to #engineering-requests with the Jira issue link and on-call Engineering Triage Lead assignment. (3) Jira issue added to the Engineering Triage board for pending review and prioritization.
expect.reason: slack-requests forwards the reacted message and thread to request-triage, which drives ChatGPT analysis, Jira issue creation (Story, Medium priority, Engineering project), a follow-up post back to #engineering-requests with the issue link and on-call lead assignment, and routing of the issue onto the Engineering Triage board — all baseline behaviors with no modifications active.

events.input: A :ticket: emoji reaction has been added to a message in the #engineering-requests Slack channel. The reacted message reads: 'We need a self-serve password reset flow for admin users — right now it requires a manual support ticket and takes 2+ days. Should support email-based reset with a 15-minute token expiry, enforce our existing password complexity rules, and invalidate all active sessions on reset.' The surrounding thread includes replies from Jordan Kim (Engineering Manager): 'This has come up in three different retros. I'm flagging it as a priority for our team — please route this directly to me and skip the usual triage queue.', and Lena Park (Frontend Engineer): 'UI mockups are already done, we just need backend support.' Message posted by Jordan Kim in thread ts=1717400000.778899 in channel #engineering-requests.

expect.action: request-triage receives the thread content from slack-requests and applies M001 (treat Jordan Kim's request differently), but since M001 specifies no alternative behavior, the system has no defined alternative process to execute; the standard pipeline therefore proceeds: chatgpt-analysis is called twice (once to analyze requirements, once to generate structured Jira content), jira-issues creates a Story with Medium priority in the Engineering project, slack-requests posts a follow-up to #engineering-requests with the Jira issue link and on-call triage lead assignment (from the active leads: Sarah Chen, Marcus Webb, Priya Nair per reference data), and jira-triage-board places the issue on the Engineering Triage board — Jordan Kim's explicit request to skip triage and route directly to him is not honored because no object in the system has behavior defined to fulfill that instruction.
expect.reason: M001 says to treat Jordan Kim's requests 'differently' but defines no alternative behavior, so no object in the system has a rule to deviate from its defined behavior, and each object executes its standard behavior chain as specified in its Behavior definition.

events.input: A :ticket: emoji reaction has been added to a message in the #engineering-requests Slack channel. The reacted message reads: 'The reporting dashboard is timing out for accounts with more than 50,000 records. We need query optimization on the reports_summary view — likely needs an index on account_id + created_at and possibly pagination on the data fetch. Finance team is blocked on end-of-month reporting.' The surrounding thread includes replies from Ravi Subramaniam (Backend Engineer): 'I can reproduce the timeout locally. Query plan shows a full table scan on reports_summary.', and Jordan Kim (Engineering Manager): 'This one is urgent — finance deadline is in 4 days. Assign this to Ravi directly and flag it on the triage board as high visibility.', and Chloe Tan (QA Lead): 'Acceptance criteria: queries under 2 seconds for accounts up to 100k records.' Message posted by Ravi Subramaniam in thread ts=1717450000.334455 in channel #engineering-requests.

expect.action: slack-requests receives the reacted message text and full surrounding thread conversation from itself (internally captured), then forwards this content to request-triage via peer message. request-triage then sends the thread content to chatgpt-analysis with a prompt to identify engineering requirements, scope, and constraints. chatgpt-analysis calls the chatgpt_analysis_data tool to retrieve data and returns analysis results to request-triage. request-triage sends a follow-up prompt to chatgpt-analysis to generate a structured Jira issue title, description, and acceptance criteria. chatgpt-analysis calls the chatgpt_analysis_data tool again and returns the structured content. request-triage sends the structured Jira content to jira-issues to create a Story with Medium priority in the Engineering project. jira-issues records the resulting issue key and link. request-triage sends the Jira issue details including link and on-call triage lead assignment instruction to slack-requests for follow-up posting, and sends a routing instruction to jira-triage-board to place the issue on the Engineering Triage board. slack-requests posts a follow-up message with the Jira issue link and assignee to #engineering-requests. jira-triage-board moves/adds the issue to the Engineering Triage board pending review column.
expect.reason: M001 specifies treating requests from Jordan Kim differently, but Jordan Kim is a thread participant not the message author (Ravi Subramaniam posted the message), and the modification lacks any specified alternative process, so M001 does not meaningfully alter the trigger condition or produce a defined alternative behavior — the standard request-triage pipeline executes unchanged per the object system definitions.

events.input: A :ticket: emoji reaction has been added to a message in the #engineering-requests Slack channel. The reacted message reads: 'We need dark mode support in the mobile app — multiple enterprise clients have requested it in their onboarding calls. Should follow the system-level preference on iOS and Android, persist user override in profile settings, and apply consistently across all screens including modals and drawers.' The surrounding thread includes replies from Priya Nair (QA Lead): 'Acceptance criteria should include automated visual regression tests for both themes.', and Marcus Webb (Backend Engineer): 'No backend changes needed — purely a frontend concern.' Message posted by Priya Nair in thread ts=1717500000.112233 in channel #engineering-requests.

expect.action: (1) Jira Story created in the Engineering project with Medium priority covering the dark mode mobile app requirements. (2) Follow-up message posted to #engineering-requests with the Jira issue link and on-call Engineering Triage Lead assignment. (3) Jira issue added to the Engineering Triage board for pending review and prioritization.
expect.reason: M001 is active but the message was posted by Priya Nair (not Jordan Kim), so the modification does not apply; the system executes baseline behavior — Jira Story creation, follow-up post to #engineering-requests, and routing to the Engineering Triage board.
