events.input: A team member has added themselves as an attendee to an existing interview session in Zapier Tables. Session ID: IS-2051, New Attendee: Carlos Mendez, Product Manager. Updated attendee list: Dr. Priya Nair (User Researcher), Jordan Kim (Design Lead), Carlos Mendez (Product Manager).

expect.action: Event discarded; no calendar update or database record written. Rejection reason logged in interview-signup-policy state: Carlos Mendez is from the Product department, which is ineligible for signup under the active M001 restriction.
expect.reason: Under M001, signups from Product, Engineering, and Marketing are rejected during this period; Carlos Mendez is a Product Manager (Product department), so interview-signup-policy discards the event and logs the reason in state without updating google-calendar or interview-sessions.

events.input: A new interview session has been created and added to Zapier Tables. Session ID: IS-2058, Scheduled Time: 2024-03-18 at 3:00 PM PST, Interview Topic: Notification Preferences Research, Interviewer: Dr. Priya Nair (User Researcher), Meeting Link: https://meet.google.com/lmn-opqr-stu, Google Calendar Event ID: gcal_event_91202.

expect.action: interview-signup-policy validates the required fields (session ID: IS-2058, scheduled time: 2024-03-18 at 3:00 PM PST, interview topic: Notification Preferences Research) and records the session internally. No Google Calendar update is sent and no confirmation is written back to interview-sessions at this stage, as those actions are only triggered upon attendee addition.
expect.reason: M001 governs attendee signups only; when a new interview session is created, interview-sessions forwards the session details to interview-signup-policy, which validates the required fields and — once validated — sends the session details to google-calendar and confirms the record back to interview-sessions, regardless of the active signup restriction.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the customer-facing feedback form that captures and forwards submitted feedback.
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted feedback entries with customer details and feedback content.
objects.behavior: When a customer submits feedback through the form, forward the complete feedback payload to the feedback-processor peer.

peers.relationship: Forward all submitted feedback data when a new submission is received

event_sources: external system: customer submits feedback form

objects.role: Business logic object that governs the feedback lifecycle — routing new submissions for storage and notification, and handling manager-composed replies to customers.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current feedback item under review, including customer contact info, feedback content, and any reply fields (subject, body, from name) filled in by the Product Manager.
objects.behavior: When a new feedback submission arrives from the feedback form, write it to the product-feedback-table and send an email notification to the product team via email-notifier simultaneously. When a Product Manager submits a reply (email subject, body, from name populated and send button clicked), send the composed reply email to the customer via customer-email-sender.

peers.relationship: Send notification with all feedback details to the Product Team when a new submission arrives
Send the composed reply email to the customer when the Product Manager triggers the send action
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Write service representing the Product Feedback Table that persists feedback rows and manager-composed reply fields.
objects.state_description: Stores all feedback rows, each containing customer info, feedback content, and optional reply fields (email subject, body, from name) added by the Product Manager.
objects.behavior: Record every feedback entry and reply-field update received. When a new row is added, emit a row-added event. Maintain the full audit trail of all feedback and associated manager responses.

peers.relationship: Forward row-added event to feedback-processor when a new feedback row is created

event_sources: external system: Product Manager fills reply fields and clicks send email button in the table

objects.role: Write service that sends email notifications to the Product Team when new feedback is received.
objects.state_description: Audit log of all notification emails sent, including timestamp, recipient, and feedback summary.
objects.behavior: When a notification request is received, send an email to the Product Team containing all provided feedback information. Record the sent notification in the audit log.

objects.role: Write service that sends reply emails from the Product Team to customers using the manager-composed subject, body, and from name.
objects.state_description: Audit log of all reply emails sent to customers, including recipient, subject, body, from name, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a reply email request is received containing subject, body, from name, and customer email address, send the email to the customer and record it in the audit log.

steps.text: Customer Sarah Mitchell submits feedback through the feedback form with the following details: Name: Sarah Mitchell, Email: sarah.mitchell@example.com, Product: Mobile App v2.3, Rating: 3/5, Feedback: 'The new navigation menu is confusing and hard to use. I often get lost trying to find the settings page. Please consider reverting to the previous layout or providing a clearer guide.'

expect.action: Email notification sent to the Product Team containing Sarah Mitchell's feedback details (name: Sarah Mitchell, email: sarah.mitchell@example.com, product: Mobile App v2.3, rating: 3/5, feedback message about confusing navigation menu), and a new row recorded in the Product Feedback Table with the same feedback information.
expect.reason: feedback-processor receives the submission from feedback-form and simultaneously writes the row to product-feedback-table and sends a notification via email-notifier to the Product Team

steps.text: Product Manager Alex Rivera fills in the reply fields for Sarah Mitchell's feedback row in the Product Feedback Table: Email Subject: 'Re: Your Feedback on Mobile App v2.3 Navigation', From Name: 'Alex Rivera, Product Team', Email Body: 'Dear Sarah, thank you for your feedback on the Mobile App v2.3 navigation. We hear your concerns and are actively working on improvements. In the meantime, we have added a quick-access guide in the Help section. We appreciate your patience and support.' Alex Rivera then clicks the send email button.
steps.source: Product Manager via Product Feedback Table

expect.action: product-feedback-table records the reply fields (email subject 'Re: Your Feedback on Mobile App v2.3 Navigation', from name 'Alex Rivera, Product Team', and the composed body message) for Sarah Mitchell's feedback row. Upon the Product Manager clicking the send button, feedback-processor sends the composed reply email to Sarah Mitchell via customer-email-sender using the provided subject, from name, and body — this is triggered by the Product Manager's send action through feedback-processor, not by a product-feedback-table event.
expect.reason: product-feedback-table emits a send-email event upon manager action, feedback-processor routes the reply fields to customer-email-sender, which sends the email to the customer

modifications.intent: When a new feedback submission arrives with a rating of 1 or 2 out of 5, send an additional urgent escalation notification email to the VP of Product (vp-product@example.com) via email-notifier with the subject 'URGENT: Low Rating Feedback Received' and the full feedback details included in the body, in addition to the standard product team notification that always goes out.

events.input: Name: Daniel Okafor, Email: daniel.okafor@example.com, Product: Desktop App v4.1, Rating: 3/5, Feedback: 'The export feature works most of the time but occasionally hangs on large files. It would be great to have a progress indicator so users know something is happening.'

expect.action: 1) Feedback entry recorded in the Product Feedback Table. 2) Email notification sent to the Product Team containing Daniel Okafor's feedback details (Name: Daniel Okafor, Email: daniel.okafor@example.com, Product: Desktop App v4.1, Rating: 3/5, Feedback: 'The export feature works most of the time but occasionally hangs on large files. It would be great to have a progress indicator so users know something is happening.')
expect.reason: On a new feedback submission, the feedback-processor writes the entry to the product-feedback-table and sends an email notification to the Product Team via email-notifier; no low-rating escalation applies as M001 is not active and the rating is 3/5.

events.input: Name: Priya Nambiar, Email: priya.nambiar@example.com, Product: Mobile App v2.3, Rating: 1/5, Feedback: 'The app crashes every time I try to open the dashboard after the latest update. I have tried reinstalling but the issue persists. This makes the app completely unusable for me.'

expect.action: 1) Feedback entry recorded in the Product Feedback Table. 2) Email notification sent to the Product Team containing Priya Nambiar's feedback details (Name: Priya Nambiar, Email: priya.nambiar@example.com, Product: Mobile App v2.3, Rating: 1/5, Feedback: 'The app crashes every time I try to open the dashboard after the latest update. I have tried reinstalling but the issue persists. This makes the app completely unusable for me.'). 3) Urgent escalation email sent to vp-product@example.com with subject 'URGENT: Low Rating Feedback Received' and full feedback details in the body.
expect.reason: M001 is active and the rating of 1/5 meets the threshold of 1 or 2 out of 5, so in addition to the standard product team notification and table write, an urgent escalation email is sent to the VP of Product at vp-product@example.com.

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes Slack message content and transforms it into a structured GitHub issue format with title, description, and labels.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-progress formatting jobs with original message content, derived title, description, and assigned labels.
objects.behavior: When a Slack message payload arrives, analyze the content to extract intent, context, and technical details. Generate a concise issue title from the core problem or request. Compose a detailed description preserving all relevant context from the thread. Classify the issue by assigning one or more labels: use 'bug' when the message describes broken or unexpected behavior, 'enhancement' when it describes a new feature or improvement request, and 'documentation' when it describes missing or incorrect docs. When multiple labels apply, include all relevant ones. After formatting is complete, send the structured issue data to github-issues and simultaneously send a confirmation summary to slack-notifications.

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes article content to extract key information and classify sentiment
objects.state_description: Tracks the current article being processed including extracted headline, summary, quotes, sentiment score, and classification
objects.behavior: When a new article arrives from rss-feed, extract the headline, summary, notable quotes, publication source, and publication date. Classify the overall sentiment as positive, negative, or neutral. Assign a confidence score. When extraction is complete, simultaneously send the structured mention record to mention-store, and send the sentiment-classified mention to sentiment-router. When a digest-generation request arrives from weekly-digest, synthesize trends and key article highlights from the data provided and return the digest content. If any processing error occurs, send an error alert to workflow-alerts.

objects.role: Business logic object that routes mentions to the appropriate Slack channels based on sentiment classification

peers.relationship: Send positive and neutral mention summaries for immediate posting to #brand-wins

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives prospect submissions from the website lead capture form and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming lead submissions with prospect details including name, company, company size, region, and contact information.
objects.behavior: When a prospect submits the lead capture form, forward the full lead payload to the lead-routing object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead submission payload when a new prospect submits the form

event_sources: webhook: lead form submitted

objects.role: Read service that retrieves qualified sales representative records from the Airtable agent roster table including region, company size, and active status.
objects.behavior: Answers queries about available sales reps filtered by region, company size, and active status. When answering queries, call the `agent_roster_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies routing rules to match incoming leads to qualified sales representatives based on region and company size.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leads with their routing status, matched rep assignments, and claim state.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives, query the agent-roster read service for active sales reps matching the lead's region and company size. When matching reps are found, notify all matched reps simultaneously via the slack-alerts write service. When a rep claims a lead via the claim event, update the lead's claim state and send the updated lead details to salesforce-leads. When no matching reps are found, log the unmatched lead in its state for review.

peers.relationship: Query for active sales reps matching the lead's region and company size when a new lead arrives
Send lead details and matched rep list to post in #new-leads channel
Send claimed lead details including rep assignment when a rep claims the lead

objects.role: Write service that posts lead alert notifications to the Slack #new-leads channel for qualified sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all Slack notifications sent including lead details, matched reps, and timestamps.
objects.behavior: When a lead alert message arrives, post it to the #new-leads channel in Slack. Record each notification in state as an audit entry.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the internal sales portal claim page where reps claim leads; receives claim actions and forwards them to lead-routing.
objects.state_description: Tracks claim events including which rep claimed which lead and when.
objects.behavior: When a sales rep submits a claim action on the agent claim page, forward the claim event including lead ID and rep ID to lead-routing via peer message. Record each claim event in state.

peers.relationship: Forward lead claim events including lead ID and rep ID when a rep claims a lead

event_sources: external system: sales rep submits lead claim via portal

objects.role: Write service that records and updates lead details in Salesforce when a rep claims a lead.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all lead records written and updated in Salesforce including claim assignments and timestamps.
objects.behavior: When lead claim details arrive, write or update the lead record in Salesforce with the assigned rep and claim status. Record each operation in state.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives sales team events from Salesforce including meetings booked and closed won deals and forwards them for ranking calculation.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming Salesforce events including event type, rep ID, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce agent event arrives such as a meeting booked or closed won deal, forward the event payload to agent-ranking.

peers.relationship: Forward agent event details including event type and rep ID when a Salesforce event arrives

event_sources: webhook: Salesforce agent event recorded (meeting booked, closed won)

objects.role: Read service that retrieves the current metric weights for meetings booked and closed won from the weighted control panel configuration.
objects.behavior: Answers queries about current weight values for ranking metrics including meetings booked and closed won. When answering queries, call the `ranking_weights_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that calculates and maintains agent ranking scores based on Salesforce events and configurable metric weights.
objects.state_description: Tracks each agent's cumulative event counts and current ranking scores indexed by rep ID.
objects.behavior: When a Salesforce agent event arrives, query ranking-weights for the current metric weights. Apply the weights to the agent's event counts to recalculate their ranking score. Update the agent's score in state. Write the updated event and score to the airtable-agent-events write service.

peers.relationship: Query for current metric weights when recalculating agent ranking scores
Send agent event details and updated ranking score to be recorded in Airtable

objects.role: Write service that records agent events including meetings booked and closed won deals into the agent events table in Airtable.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all agent events written to Airtable including event type, rep ID, score, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an agent event record arrives, write it to the agent events table in Airtable. Record each write operation in state.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the weighted control panel where admins adjust metric weights for agent ranking.
objects.state_description: Tracks weight update events including which metrics were adjusted and their new values.
objects.behavior: When an admin submits updated weights via the control panel, forward the new weight configuration to agent-ranking.

peers.relationship: Forward updated metric weight configuration when an admin adjusts weights via the control panel

event_sources: external system: admin updates metric weights via weighted control panel

steps.text: Prospect Maria Gonzalez submits the lead capture form on the website with the following details: name: Maria Gonzalez, email: maria.gonzalez@techcorp.com, company: TechCorp, company size: Enterprise, region: West Coast, phone: 415-555-0192.

expect.action: Slack message posted to #new-leads channel notifying matched sales reps of new lead from Maria Gonzalez at TechCorp (Enterprise, West Coast).
expect.reason: lead-capture-form forwards the lead payload to lead-routing, which queries agent-roster for matching active reps by region and company size, then instructs slack-alerts to post a notification to #new-leads.

steps.text: Sales rep Jordan Kim (rep ID: rep-204) submits a claim on lead ID lead-8821 (Maria Gonzalez, TechCorp) via the agent claim page in the internal sales portal.

expect.action: Lead record for Maria Gonzalez (lead-8821) written/updated in Salesforce with assigned rep Jordan Kim and claim status set to claimed.
expect.reason: lead-claim-portal forwards the claim event to lead-routing, which sends claim details to salesforce-leads to update the lead record in Salesforce.

steps.text: Salesforce records a 'meeting booked' event for sales rep Jordan Kim (rep ID: rep-204), associated with lead TechCorp / Maria Gonzalez, event timestamp: 2024-06-10T14:00:00Z.

expect.action: Agent event record for Jordan Kim (meeting booked, TechCorp/Maria Gonzalez) written to the agent events table in Airtable, and Jordan Kim's ranking score recalculated and updated.
expect.reason: salesforce-events forwards the event to agent-ranking, which queries ranking-weights, recalculates the score, and instructs airtable-agent-events to write the event record.

steps.text: Admin Taylor Reyes submits updated metric weights via the weighted control panel: meetings booked weight = 0.4, closed won weight = 0.6.

expect.action: weight-control-panel forwards the new weight configuration (meetings_booked: 0.4, closed_won: 0.6) to agent-ranking. Agent-ranking receives and stores the updated weights for use in future ranking calculations. No ranking scores are recalculated and no further downstream actions occur at this time. (Reason: weight-control-panel forwards the new weight configuration to agent-ranking, which stores the updated weights; ranking recalculation only occurs when a Salesforce agent event arrives, not when weights are updated.)
expect.reason: weight-control-panel forwards the new weight configuration to agent-ranking, which applies the updated weights to recalculate agent ranking scores.

modifications.intent: When a new lead arrives with company size Enterprise and region East Coast, only notify sales reps who are both active and have a current ranking score strictly greater than 80. Reps with a ranking score of 80 or below must be excluded from the matched rep list for East Coast Enterprise leads, even if they match on region and company size.

events.input: New lead submission: name: David Park, email: david.park@apexsystems.com, company: ApexSystems, company size: Enterprise, region: East Coast, phone: 212-555-0347. Prospect is interested in an enterprise contract for cloud infrastructure services.

expect.action: Notification posted to the #new-leads Slack channel alerting Jordan Kim and Priya Nair of the new lead from David Park at ApexSystems (Enterprise, East Coast). Note: No East Coast Enterprise reps exist in the roster; the agent-roster returns no matching reps for East Coast Enterprise, so the lead is logged as unmatched with no Slack notification sent.
expect.reason: Lead-routing queries agent-roster for active reps matching East Coast and Enterprise; no reps in the roster match both region=East Coast and company_size_focus=Enterprise, so lead-routing logs the unmatched lead in state and no Slack notification is sent.

events.input: New lead submission: name: Sandra Wu, email: sandra.wu@nexuscorp.com, company: NexusCorp, company size: Enterprise, region: East Coast, phone: 646-555-0821. Prospect submitted via the website enterprise inquiry form requesting a demo and pricing for a 500-seat deployment.

expect.action: The unmatched lead for Sandra Wu at NexusCorp (Enterprise, East Coast) is logged in lead-routing state for review, with no Slack notification sent.
expect.reason: Under M001, only active reps with a ranking score strictly greater than 80 are notified for East Coast Enterprise leads; no reps in the roster match region=East Coast and Enterprise company size, so no qualifying reps are found and the lead is logged as unmatched.

events.input: New lead submission: name: Carlos Rivera, email: carlos.rivera@brightwave.io, company: BrightWave, company size: Mid-Market, region: West Coast, phone: 415-555-0763. Prospect is evaluating CRM integration solutions for a 120-person sales team.

expect.action: Notification posted to the #new-leads Slack channel alerting Jordan Kim and Priya Nair of the new lead from Carlos Rivera at BrightWave (Mid-Market, West Coast).
expect.reason: Lead-routing queries agent-roster for active reps matching West Coast and Mid-Market; both Jordan Kim (rep-204) and Priya Nair (rep-117) are active West Coast reps with Mid-Market in their company_size_focus, so slack-alerts posts a notification to #new-leads for both reps simultaneously. M001 only applies to East Coast Enterprise leads and does not affect this routing.

events.input: Salesforce event: event type: closed won, rep ID: rep-317, rep name: Alex Torres, associated company: NexusCorp, deal value: Enterprise tier, event timestamp: 2024-06-18T15:45:00Z. Deal closed after three-week negotiation cycle.

expect.action: Agent event record for rep-317 (Alex Torres), event type closed won, associated company NexusCorp, written to the agent events table in Airtable.
expect.reason: Salesforce-events forwards the closed won event to agent-ranking, which queries ranking-weights, recalculates Alex Torres's ranking score, updates it in state, and writes the updated event and score to airtable-agent-events, which records it in the Airtable agent events table.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new leave request submissions from Zapier Interfaces and forwards them into the workflow.
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted leave requests with employee ID, leave type, dates, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new leave request submission event arrives from Zapier Interfaces, forward the full request payload to the leave-approval-policy object. Record the submission in local state.

peers.relationship: Forward full leave request payload when a new submission event arrives

event_sources: webhook: leave request submitted via Zapier Interfaces

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives manager approval or rejection decisions from Zapier Tables and forwards them into the workflow.
objects.state_description: Tracks manager decisions with request ID, manager ID, decision (approved/rejected), and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a manager approval or rejection event arrives from Zapier Tables, forward the decision payload to the leave-approval-policy object. Record the decision in local state.

peers.relationship: Forward manager decision payload when an approval or rejection event arrives

event_sources: webhook: manager decision recorded in Zapier Tables

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces leave approval rules, validates balances, determines notification targets, and orchestrates downstream updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leave requests under review, including request ID, employee ID, leave type, requested days, current status, and any validation outcomes.
objects.behavior: When a new leave request arrives: validate that the leave type is one of Annual Leave, Sick Leave, Unpaid Leave, or Maternity Leave; query leave-balance-store for the employee's current balance for the requested leave type; if balance is insufficient for types that require it (Annual Leave), flag the request as invalid and notify the employee; otherwise mark the request as pending and write it to leave-request-store; simultaneously send submission notification instructions to email-notifications targeting both the employee and the relevant manager. When a manager decision arrives: retrieve the original request from leave-request-store; if approved, calculate the number of days consumed and send a balance deduction update to leave-balance-store; update the request status in leave-request-store; simultaneously send approval or rejection notification instructions to email-notifications targeting both the employee and the manager.

peers.relationship: Query current leave balance for an employee and leave type before approving; send balance deduction update when a request is approved
Write new pending leave requests on submission; update request status on manager decision
Send notification instructions for submission, approval, and rejection events targeting employee and manager

objects.role: Dual-role read/write service object that stores employee leave balances per leave type and answers balance lookup queries.
objects.state_description: Stores per-employee leave balances keyed by employee ID and leave type (Annual Leave, Sick Leave, Unpaid Leave, Maternity Leave), including configured initial balances and current remaining balances.
objects.behavior: When a balance lookup query arrives, call the leave_balance_store_data tool to retrieve the current balance for the specified employee and leave type, then reply with the result. When a balance deduction update arrives, record the updated remaining balance for the specified employee and leave type. When admin configuration events arrive specifying initial balances, store them against the appropriate employee and leave type. When answering queries, call the `leave_balance_store_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object that persists leave requests and their status changes for manager review and audit purposes.
objects.state_description: Stores all leave requests with fields: request ID, employee ID, leave type, start date, end date, days requested, submission timestamp, status (pending/approved/rejected), and manager decision timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new pending leave request record arrives, store it with status set to pending. When a status update arrives for an existing request, update the status and record the decision timestamp. Maintain the full history of all requests as an audit trail.

objects.role: Write service object that sends email notifications to employees and managers upon leave request submission, approval, or rejection.
objects.state_description: Tracks sent notification records with recipient, event type, request ID, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When notification instructions arrive specifying recipients, event type (submitted, approved, rejected), and request details, send the appropriate email to each recipient and record the notification in local state.

steps.text: A new leave request has been submitted via Zapier Interfaces. Employee: Sarah Mitchell, Marketing Coordinator. Leave type: Annual Leave. Start date: 2024-02-12. End date: 2024-02-16. Duration: 5 days. Reason: Family vacation. Manager: David Chen, Marketing Manager. Employee email: sarah.mitchell@company.com. Manager email: david.chen@company.com.

expect.action: Leave request stored as pending in leave-request-store for Sarah Mitchell (Annual Leave, 2024-02-12 to 2024-02-16). Submission notification email sent to sarah.mitchell@company.com and david.chen@company.com confirming the request has been received and is awaiting manager review.
expect.reason: leave-approval-policy validates the leave type (Annual Leave is valid), queries leave-balance-store for Sarah Mitchell's balance, stores the pending request via leave-request-store, and triggers email-notifications to send submission emails to the employee and manager.

steps.text: Manager David Chen has recorded a decision in Zapier Tables for Sarah Mitchell's leave request (Annual Leave, 2024-02-12 to 2024-02-16). Decision: Approved. Manager email: david.chen@company.com. Employee email: sarah.mitchell@company.com. Request ID: LR-2024-0112.

expect.action: Leave balance for Sarah Mitchell deducted by 5 days for Annual Leave in leave-balance-store. Leave request LR-2024-0112 status updated to approved in leave-request-store. Approval notification email sent to sarah.mitchell@company.com and david.chen@company.com confirming the leave request has been approved.
expect.reason: leave-approval-policy processes the approval decision, instructs leave-balance-store to deduct the 5-day balance, updates the request status via leave-request-store, and triggers email-notifications to send approval emails to the employee and manager.

modifications.intent: For the next little while, hold off on processing any new Annual Leave requests right away — just let them sit until things settle down a bit.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the company intranet chatbot interface that receives employee questions and button-click actions
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming employee questions, generated responses, and button-click events (copy or log-to-sheet)
objects.behavior: When an employee submits a question via the chatbot interface, forward it to the onboarding-knowledge object for processing. When an employee clicks the 'copy' button, handle the copy action locally. When an employee clicks the 'log question' button, forward the question and response details to the question-logger object.

peers.relationship: Forward employee question when submitted via chatbot
Forward question and response details when the log-question button is clicked

event_sources: user action: employee submits question via chatbot
user action: employee clicks log-question button

objects.role: Business logic object that applies onboarding domain knowledge to answer employee questions using the configured knowledge base sources
objects.state_description: Tracks current conversation context, pending queries, and assembled responses for active employee sessions
objects.behavior: When an employee question arrives, determine which knowledge sources are relevant (handbook, benefits, policies). Query the employee-handbook, benefits-overview, and company-policies read services as needed. Synthesize a coherent, accurate answer using the retrieved content. When the question relates to HR policies, prioritize company-policies data. When the question relates to benefits, prioritize benefits-overview data. When the question relates to general onboarding procedures, prioritize employee-handbook data. Send the composed response back to the intranet-chatbot for display to the employee.

peers.relationship: Query for onboarding procedures and general HR information when employee question is relevant
Query for benefits details when employee question is relevant
Query for policy details when employee question is relevant
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Read service representing the Employee Handbook PDF knowledge base used to answer onboarding and HR procedure questions
objects.behavior: Responds to queries about onboarding procedures, HR policies, and general employment information sourced from the Employee Handbook PDF. When answering queries, call the `employee_handbook_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Read service representing the Benefits Overview spreadsheet used to answer questions about employee benefits
objects.behavior: Responds to queries about employee benefits, coverage details, enrollment procedures, and compensation packages sourced from the Benefits Overview spreadsheet. When answering queries, call the `benefits_overview_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Read service representing the Company Policies web page used to answer questions about workplace rules and compliance
objects.behavior: Responds to queries about company rules, compliance requirements, code of conduct, and workplace policies sourced from the Company Policies web page. When answering queries, call the `company_policies_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that records employee questions and chatbot responses to a Google Sheets tracking sheet for HR analytics
objects.state_description: Audit trail of logged questions including employee identifier, question text, chatbot response, timestamp, and session metadata
objects.behavior: When a log-question event arrives containing question text, response text, and session metadata, record the entry to the Google Sheets tracking sheet. Append a new row with the employee identifier, question, response, and timestamp.

steps.text: New employee Sarah Chen submits the question 'How many vacation days do I get in my first year?' via the company intranet chatbot during onboarding.

expect.action: Response delivered to Sarah Chen via the intranet chatbot stating the number of vacation days in the first year, sourced from the Employee Handbook PDF and/or Benefits Overview spreadsheet knowledge base.
expect.reason: intranet-chatbot forwards the question to onboarding-knowledge, which queries employee-handbook and benefits-overview, then returns the answer back to intranet-chatbot for display to the employee.

steps.text: New employee Sarah Chen clicks the 'log question' button in the intranet chatbot after receiving the answer to 'How many vacation days do I get in my first year?', with response text 'Full-time employees receive 10 vacation days in their first year of employment.' and session ID 'session-20240315-sc001'.

expect.action: A new row appended to the Google Sheets tracking sheet recording employee Sarah Chen, question 'How many vacation days do I get in my first year?', response 'Full-time employees receive 10 vacation days in their first year of employment.', and session ID 'session-20240315-sc001'.
expect.reason: intranet-chatbot forwards the log-question event to question-logger, which appends the entry to the Google Sheets tracking sheet.

modifications.intent: Questions that mention both benefits and policies should lean toward the policy side of things when there's any overlap — benefits details matter less than making sure people know what they're actually required to follow.

events.input: New employee Marcus Webb asks via the intranet chatbot: 'What health insurance plans are available to me and when do I need to enroll by?' Session ID: session-20240318-mw002

expect.action: Response sent to Marcus Webb (session-20240318-mw002) via the intranet chatbot interface stating that the available health insurance plans are PPO, HMO, and HDHP, the employer covers 80% of medical premiums, and the open enrollment period runs November 1–November 30.
expect.reason: No modifications are active; onboarding-knowledge prioritizes benefits-overview data for benefits questions and synthesizes an answer about available health plans and enrollment deadlines, which intranet-chatbot displays to the employee.

events.input: New employee Marcus Webb asks via the intranet chatbot: 'Am I required to enroll in a health insurance plan, and what happens if I miss the enrollment window — is there a penalty or a waiting period before I can sign up again?' Session ID: session-20240318-mw003

expect.action: intranet-chatbot displays a response to Marcus Webb indicating that the available knowledge sources do not contain specific information about mandatory health insurance enrollment requirements or penalties/waiting periods for missing the open enrollment window. The response may reference that open enrollment runs November 1–November 30 (from benefits-overview_data) and that the employer contributes 80% to medical plans, but cannot confirm whether enrollment is mandatory or detail consequences of missing the window, as no such policy data exists in employee-handbook, benefits-overview, or company-policies sources. Given M001, onboarding-knowledge leans toward company-policies data, but company-policies_data contains no sections addressing mandatory enrollment or missed-enrollment penalties either.
expect.reason: onboarding-knowledge queries all three read services (employee-handbook, benefits-overview, company-policies) and synthesizes a response, but none of the reference data contains information about mandatory health insurance enrollment or penalties/waiting periods for missing the enrollment window, so the terminal output is a partial/fallback response referencing only what is available (open enrollment period: November 1–November 30, employer contribution 80%) while acknowledging the specific questions about mandatory enrollment and penalties cannot be answered from the available knowledge base.

events.input: New employee Priya Nair asks via the intranet chatbot: 'What is the dress code policy for remote employees?' Session ID: session-20240318-pn004

expect.action: intranet-chatbot displays a response to Priya Nair indicating that no dress code policy information is available for remote employees. The onboarding-knowledge object queries company-policies (prioritized due to M001 policy-lean and the policy nature of the question), employee-handbook, and/or benefits-overview as relevant, but none of the reference data contains any dress code policy content. The synthesized response communicated back to intranet-chatbot for display will reflect that no relevant dress code information was found in the available knowledge sources.
expect.reason: The onboarding-knowledge object queries the relevant read services (prioritizing company-policies per M001 since the question relates to workplace policy), but none of the reference data — company-policies, employee-handbook, or benefits-overview — contains any dress code policy content, so the synthesized response returned to intranet-chatbot must acknowledge the absence of that information.

events.input: New employee Marcus Webb clicks the 'log question' button after receiving his answer about mandatory health insurance enrollment and re-enrollment waiting periods. Question: 'Am I required to enroll in a health insurance plan, and what happens if I miss the enrollment window — is there a penalty or a waiting period before I can sign up again?' Response: 'Per company policy, health insurance enrollment is mandatory for all full-time employees. If you miss the 30-day new-hire enrollment window, you must wait until the next open enrollment period, which occurs annually in November. No penalty applies, but you will be without coverage in the interim.' Session ID: session-20240318-mw003

expect.action: New row appended to the Google Sheets tracking sheet with Marcus Webb's employee identifier, question ('Am I required to enroll in a health insurance plan, and what happens if I miss the enrollment window — is there a penalty or a waiting period before I can sign up again?'), response ('Per company policy, health insurance enrollment is mandatory for all full-time employees. If you miss the 30-day new-hire enrollment window, you must wait until the next open enrollment period, which occurs annually in November. No penalty applies, but you will be without coverage in the interim.'), session ID session-20240318-mw003, and timestamp.
expect.reason: When an employee clicks the 'log question' button, intranet-chatbot forwards the question and response details to question-logger, which appends a new row to the Google Sheets tracking sheet with the employee identifier, question, response, and timestamp.

name: Jira Epic to GitLab Release Notes

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Jira workspace for epic status changes to Done and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming Jira issue status-change webhook payloads received since startup.
objects.behavior: When a Jira issue status-change event arrives, forward the full event payload to the release-notes-generator peer. Only forward events where the issue type is Epic and the new status is Done.

peers.relationship: Forward epic-completed event payload when an epic issue transitions to Done status

event_sources: webhook: Jira issue status changed

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes completed Jira epics and orchestrates generation, publication, and notification of release notes.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current epic being processed including epic key, title, description, assignee, generated release notes content, branch name, merge request URL, and processing stage.
objects.behavior: When an epic-completed event arrives, verify the issue is of type Epic and status is Done. Use the generate-markdown-release-notes skill to call OpenAI and produce professional Markdown release notes from the epic details. Construct the branch name as release-notes/epic-update. Send the generated content and branch name to gitlab-repo to create the branch, fetch CHANGELOG.md, merge new content at the top, commit with a message referencing the epic key and title, and open a merge request targeting main. Simultaneously look up the epic assignee's Slack profile via the slack-directory read service. Once the merge request URL is available and the assignee Slack user ID is resolved, send a direct Slack message via slack-notifications to the assignee requesting review of the merge request.

peers.relationship: Send branch name, generated release notes content, epic key, and epic title to create branch, update CHANGELOG.md, commit, and open a merge request
Look up the Slack profile of the epic assignee by name or email to resolve their Slack user ID
Send a direct Slack message to the epic assignee with the merge request URL requesting review

objects.role: Write service object representing the GitLab repository that creates branches, updates CHANGELOG.md, commits changes, and opens merge requests.
objects.state_description: Records of branch creation events, CHANGELOG.md fetch-and-update operations, commits made, and merge requests opened, keyed by epic key.
objects.behavior: When a request arrives with branch name, release notes content, epic key, and epic title: create the specified branch from main, fetch the current CHANGELOG.md from main, prepend the new release notes content to the existing CHANGELOG.md, commit the updated file with a descriptive message referencing the epic key and title, and open a merge request targeting main. Record each operation in state.

objects.role: Read service object that looks up Slack user profiles by name or email to resolve Slack user IDs.
objects.behavior: When answering queries, call the slack_directory_data tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object that sends direct Slack messages to specified users.
objects.state_description: Log of direct messages sent including recipient user ID, message content, timestamp, and associated epic key.
objects.behavior: When a send-direct-message request arrives with a Slack user ID and message content, deliver the direct message to the specified user via Slack. Record the delivery in state.

steps.text: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-142 – Mobile Payment Gateway Integration' assigned to Sofia Reyes (sofia.reyes@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Mobile Payment Gateway Integration. Epic key: PROJ-142.

expect.action: A direct Slack message is sent to Sofia Reyes requesting review of the release notes merge request for PROJ-142 – Mobile Payment Gateway Integration, including a link to the GitLab merge request targeting the main branch.
expect.reason: jira-events forwards the epic-completed event to release-notes-generator, which generates release notes via OpenAI, instructs gitlab-repo to create the release-notes/epic-update branch, prepend CHANGELOG.md, commit, and open a merge request, then looks up Sofia Reyes via slack-directory and sends her a direct message via slack-notifications.

modifications.intent: When a completed epic is assigned to Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@company.com), send the merge request review request to team lead Diana Cho (slack user ID: U04DIANACH0) instead of to Marcus directly.

events.input: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-118 – Offline Mode for iOS App' assigned to Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Offline Mode for iOS App. Epic key: PROJ-118. Description: Implemented full offline data sync capability for the iOS mobile application, including local SQLite caching, conflict resolution on reconnect, and background sync scheduling. Acceptance criteria met: all 14 sub-tasks closed, QA sign-off received on 2024-03-10.

expect.action: 1) CHANGELOG.md updated in GitLab repository on branch release-notes/epic-update with a new merge request targeting main, referencing PROJ-118 – Offline Mode for iOS App. 2) Direct Slack message sent to Marcus Webb requesting review of the merge request.
expect.reason: The baseline behavior has release-notes-generator create a GitLab branch, update CHANGELOG.md, open a merge request, and send a DM to the epic assignee (Marcus Webb) with the merge request URL; M001 is not yet active at E001's timestamp.

events.input: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-131 – Offline Mode for Android App' assigned to Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Offline Mode for Android App. Epic key: PROJ-131. Description: Delivered full offline data synchronization for the Android mobile application, covering Room database caching, merge conflict resolution on reconnect, and WorkManager-based background sync. All 11 sub-tasks closed, QA sign-off received on 2024-03-14.

expect.action: 1) CHANGELOG.md updated in GitLab repository on branch release-notes/epic-update with a new merge request targeting main, referencing PROJ-131 – Offline Mode for Android App. 2) Direct Slack message sent to Diana Cho requesting review of the merge request.
expect.reason: M001 is active at E002's timestamp and specifies that when the completed epic is assigned to Marcus Webb, the merge request review request is sent to Diana Cho (Slack user ID: U04DIANACH0) instead of Marcus directly.

events.input: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-145 – Multi-Currency Support for Checkout' assigned to Sofia Reyes (sofia.reyes@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Multi-Currency Support for Checkout. Epic key: PROJ-145. Description: Extended the checkout pipeline to support 12 additional currencies with real-time exchange rate lookups via the ECB API, locale-aware formatting, and per-region tax rule enforcement. All 9 sub-tasks closed, QA sign-off received on 2024-03-15.

expect.action: 1) CHANGELOG.md updated in GitLab repository on branch release-notes/epic-update with a new merge request targeting main, referencing PROJ-145 – Multi-Currency Support for Checkout. 2) Direct Slack message sent to Sofia Reyes requesting review of the merge request.
expect.reason: M001 only redirects notifications for epics assigned to Marcus Webb; PROJ-145 is assigned to Sofia Reyes, so baseline behavior applies and the DM goes to Sofia Reyes (Slack user ID: U04SRFX29).

events.input: Jira issue status changed: Epic 'PROJ-152 – Push Notification Infrastructure Overhaul' assigned to Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@company.com) has been moved to status Done. Issue type: Epic. Summary: Push Notification Infrastructure Overhaul. Epic key: PROJ-152. Description: Replaced legacy push notification service with Firebase Cloud Messaging across iOS and Android, added per-user notification preference management, delivery receipts, and retry logic for failed deliveries. All 17 sub-tasks closed, QA sign-off received on 2024-03-18.

expect.action: 1) CHANGELOG.md updated in GitLab repository on branch release-notes/epic-update with a new merge request targeting main, referencing PROJ-152 – Push Notification Infrastructure Overhaul. 2) Direct Slack message sent to Diana Cho requesting review of the merge request.
expect.reason: M001 is active at E004's timestamp and the epic is assigned to Marcus Webb, so the merge request review request is redirected to Diana Cho (Slack user ID: U04DIANACH0) instead of Marcus.

objects.role: Entry-point service representing the Team Dashboard Interface that captures the Account Executive's 'Generate call prep' button click event
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming button-click events including the Account Executive identity and associated customer context
objects.behavior: When a 'Generate call prep' button click event arrives from the Team Dashboard Interface, forward the event payload (Account Executive identity, customer identifier) to the call-prep-workflow business logic object via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward the Account Executive identity and customer identifier when a 'Generate call prep' button click is received

event_sources: webhook: generate_call_prep button click event from Team Dashboard Interface

objects.role: Read service representing the Zapier Customers Table that retrieves customer records on demand
objects.behavior: Serves as the authoritative source for customer data including contact details, history, and account information. When answering queries, call the `customers_table_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates call preparation by retrieving customer data, generating insights, and coordinating document creation and notification
objects.state_description: Tracks in-progress call prep requests including Account Executive identity, customer identifier, retrieved customer data, and generated insights
objects.behavior: When a call prep request arrives with an Account Executive identity and customer identifier, query the customers-table read service for the full customer record. Once customer data is available, apply AI analysis to generate call preparation insights including key talking points, recent activity, account health, and recommended next steps. Then send the customer data and generated insights to google-docs for document creation, and send the Account Executive email address and the call prep content summary to email-sender simultaneously.

peers.relationship: Query for full customer record using the customer identifier from the trigger event
Send generated call prep insights and customer context to create a new Google Doc
Send Account Executive email address and Google Doc link request to deliver the final notification

objects.role: Write service representing Google Docs that creates new documents containing call prep information and returns document links
objects.state_description: Tracks created Google Docs with document IDs, titles, content, and creation timestamps
objects.behavior: When call prep content is received, create a new Google Doc with the provided insights and customer information. Record the document ID and shareable link. Reply with the generated document URL so callers can include it in downstream notifications.

peers.relationship: Send the generated Google Doc URL so it can be included in the Account Executive notification email

objects.role: Write service representing an email delivery system that sends the Account Executive a link to the generated Google Doc
objects.state_description: Tracks sent emails with recipient, subject, body, Google Doc link, and send timestamp
objects.behavior: When an email request arrives containing an Account Executive email address and a Google Doc URL, compose and send an email to the Account Executive with the link to the generated call prep Google Doc. Record the sent email details.

steps.text: Account Executive Sarah Mitchell clicks the 'Generate call prep' button on the Customers page for customer Pinnacle Solutions (customer ID: CUST-8821) in the Team Dashboard Interface. Sarah Mitchell's email is sarah.mitchell@company.com.

expect.action: Email sent to sarah.mitchell@company.com containing a link to the newly created Google Doc with call prep insights for Pinnacle Solutions.
expect.reason: team-dashboard forwards the event to call-prep-workflow, which retrieves customer data from customers-table, generates insights, creates a Google Doc via google-docs, and triggers email-sender to deliver the doc link to the Account Executive.

modifications.intent: Starting today, only process call prep requests during business hours (Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM). Any request received outside of business hours should be queued and processed at the start of the next business day.

events.input: Account Executive James Hartley clicks the 'Generate call prep' button on the Customers page for customer Meridian Logistics (customer ID: CUST-4472) in the Team Dashboard Interface. James Hartley's email is james.hartley@company.com. Customer context: Meridian Logistics is a mid-market account up for renewal next month with a history of support escalations.

expect.action: call-prep-workflow queries customers-table for CUST-4472; customers-table calls the customers_table_data tool and returns data (no matching record for CUST-4472 exists in the reference data, but the tool is still called). Assuming the tool returns whatever data is available, call-prep-workflow generates AI insights, then simultaneously sends customer data and insights to google-docs (which creates a Google Doc and returns a URL), and sends james.hartley@company.com plus the call prep content summary to email-sender. email-sender then sends an email to james.hartley@company.com with the Google Doc link.
expect.reason: The event occurs at W02-5T14:30, before modification M001 activates at W03-1T09:00, so baseline behavior applies: team-dashboard forwards the event to call-prep-workflow, which queries customers-table, generates insights, and coordinates google-docs and email-sender in parallel, with email-sender delivering the doc link to james.hartley@company.com.

events.input: Account Executive Rachel Nguyen clicks the 'Generate call prep' button on the Customers page for customer Apex Dynamics (customer ID: CUST-5590) in the Team Dashboard Interface. Rachel Nguyen's email is rachel.nguyen@company.com. Customer context: Apex Dynamics is a high-value enterprise account that recently expanded their contract and has an upcoming executive business review scheduled for next week.

expect.action: The call prep request for Apex Dynamics (CUST-5590) is queued and will be processed at the start of the next business day (Monday 9 AM).
expect.reason: M001 is active and requires that call prep requests received outside business hours (Monday–Friday, 9 AM–5 PM) be queued rather than processed immediately; W03-1T22:15 is outside business hours, so the request is held until the next business day.

events.input: Account Executive Carlos Vega clicks the 'Generate call prep' button on the Customers page for customer Brightfield Partners (customer ID: CUST-7103) in the Team Dashboard Interface. Carlos Vega's email is carlos.vega@company.com. Customer context: Brightfield Partners is a new logo acquired last quarter and is entering their first quarterly business review cycle with a strong upsell opportunity identified by the sales team.

expect.action: (1) A new Google Doc is created containing call prep insights and customer information for CUST-7103 (Arcadia Financial Group). (2) An email is sent to marcus.webb@company.com (the Account Executive on record for CUST-7103) with a link to the generated Google Doc.
expect.reason: W03-2T10:45 falls within business hours under M001, so call-prep-workflow processes the request normally: it retrieves the customer record for CUST-7103, generates insights, creates a Google Doc, and sends an email with the doc link to Carlos Vega.

events.input: Account Executive Sarah Mitchell clicks the 'Generate call prep' button on the Customers page for customer Pinnacle Solutions (customer ID: CUST-8821) in the Team Dashboard Interface. Sarah Mitchell's email is sarah.mitchell@company.com. Customer context: Pinnacle Solutions is a long-standing enterprise customer with a scheduled renewal discussion and a recently opened support ticket regarding API integration performance.

expect.action: (1) A new Google Doc is created containing call prep insights and customer information for Pinnacle Solutions (CUST-8821). (2) An email is sent to sarah.mitchell@company.com with a link to the generated Google Doc.
expect.reason: W03-2T15:00 falls within business hours under M001, so call-prep-workflow processes the request normally: it retrieves the customer record for CUST-8821, generates insights, creates a Google Doc, and sends an email with the doc link to Sarah Mitchell.

objects.role: Entry-point service representing the Zapier Interfaces OOO status form that captures the user's out-of-office activation trigger.
objects.state_description: Records submitted OOO form entries including submitter identity and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an OOO form submission arrives, forward the event to the ooo-status-policy business logic object. Record each submission in state.

peers.relationship: Forward OOO activation event with submitter details when a form is submitted.

event_sources: webhook: Zapier Interfaces form submission

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the #general Slack channel, both receiving outbound notifications and forwarding inbound Reacji (emoji reaction) events.
objects.state_description: Tracks inbound Reacji events (message ID, emoji, reactor, timestamp) and outbound notifications sent to the channel.
objects.behavior: When a Reacji (emoji reaction) event arrives on a message in #general, forward it to the task-tracker business logic object via peer message. When a notification message is received from a peer, post it to the #general Slack channel.

peers.relationship: Forward Reacji event details (message ID, emoji, reactor, original message content, timestamp) when an emoji reaction is detected on a message.

event_sources: external system: Slack Reacji (emoji reaction) on #general message

objects.role: Write service representing the Engineering Team Slack channel that receives OOO status notifications.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of OOO notifications sent to the Engineering Team, including message content and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification message is received, post it to the Engineering Team Slack channel and record it in state.

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces OOO activation rules, notifies the Engineering Team, and activates the task-tracking system.
objects.state_description: Tracks current OOO status (active/inactive), the active user's identity, and activation timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an OOO activation event arrives from the form: set OOO status to active for the submitting user, compose an Engineering Team notification message announcing the user's absence, and activate task tracking mode. Send the notification to engineering-team-slack and the activation signal to task-tracker simultaneously.

peers.relationship: Send OOO notification message to the Engineering Team when OOO is activated.
Send OOO activation signal with user identity when OOO status becomes active.

objects.role: Business logic object that manages task creation, status transitions, and requester notifications for tasks submitted during OOO absence.
objects.state_description: Tracks whether OOO mode is active, pending task queue with task ID, original message, requester, emoji trigger, status (Pending/In Progress/Complete), and last updated timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an OOO activation signal arrives, enable task-capture mode. When a Reacji event arrives while OOO mode is active, create a new task record with status Pending, capturing the original message, requester identity, emoji, and timestamp, then write it to task-store. When a task status update arrives from the ooo-dashboard (Pending, In Progress, or Complete), update the task record in task-store and compose a status notification to the original requester, then send the notification to requester-notifier. When OOO mode is deactivated, disable task-capture mode.

peers.relationship: Write new task records when Reacji events are captured and update existing task records when status changes.
Send requester notification with updated task status when a task status change is processed.
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Dual-role write and read service representing the Zapier Tables database that stores tasks and supports dashboard queries.
objects.state_description: Database of task records with fields: task ID, original message, requester, emoji trigger, status (Pending/In Progress/Complete), created timestamp, updated timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new task record is received, insert it into the Zapier Tables database and record it in state. When a task update is received, update the matching record in the database. When answering queries, call the task_store_data tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Zapier Interfaces dashboard that displays accumulated tasks and captures status update actions from the returning user.
objects.state_description: Tracks dashboard view sessions and status update actions submitted by the user (task ID, new status, timestamp).
objects.behavior: When a status update action is submitted by the user on the dashboard (setting a task to Pending, In Progress, or Complete), forward the update event to task-tracker. When a dashboard view request arrives, retrieve current tasks from task-store and render them for the user.

peers.relationship: Forward task status update events (task ID, new status) when the user updates a task on the dashboard.
Query task records to populate the dashboard view when a user accesses the dashboard.

event_sources: external system: Zapier Interfaces dashboard status update action

objects.role: Write service representing the Slack notification system that sends direct messages or channel messages to original task requesters about status updates.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of requester notifications sent, including recipient, task ID, status, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a requester notification is received, send a Slack message to the original requester with the task status update and record it in state.

steps.text: Sarah Chen, Software Engineer, submits the OOO status form indicating she will be out of office starting Monday June 9 through Friday June 13, 2025.

expect.action: Notification posted to Engineering Team Slack channel announcing Sarah Chen's absence from June 9 to June 13, 2025; task-capture mode activated in task-tracker.
expect.reason: ooo-form forwards submission to ooo-status-policy, which notifies engineering-team-slack and activates task-tracker via peer messages.

steps.text: User @marcus_lee reacts with a 📋 emoji (Reacji) to a message in #general from @priya_patel (message text: 'Can someone review the API rate-limiting PR #482 when they get a chance?', message timestamp: 2025-06-10T14:23:00Z, channel: #general).

expect.action: New task record inserted into Zapier Tables database with status Pending, capturing original message 'Can someone review the API rate-limiting PR #482 when they get a chance?', Reacji by @marcus_lee, timestamp 2025-06-10T14:23:00Z, and status Pending.
expect.reason: slack-general-channel forwards Reacji event to task-tracker, which creates a new task record and writes it to task-store via peer message.

steps.text: Sarah Chen returns from OOO and updates task for 'API rate-limiting PR #482 review' (task ID: TASK-001) to status 'In Progress' via the Zapier Interfaces dashboard.
steps.source: Zapier Interfaces dashboard status update action

expect.action: ooo-dashboard forwards the status update for TASK-001 to task-tracker; task-tracker updates TASK-001 in task-store to status 'In Progress'; task-tracker then sends a requester notification to requester-notifier, which sends a Slack message to Sarah Chen (the requester of TASK-001) notifying her that the task associated with 'API rate-limiting PR #482 review' (original message: 'OOO status form submitted: Sarah Chen will be out of office starting Monday June 9 through Friday June 13, 2025.') has been updated to status 'In Progress'.
expect.reason: ooo-dashboard forwards status update to task-tracker, which updates task-store and triggers requester-notifier to send a Slack message to the original requester @priya_patel.

modifications.intent: Automatically disable task-capture mode at exactly 5:00 PM on Friday June 13, 2025, even if no manual OOO deactivation signal has been received. Any Reacji events arriving after 5:00 PM on June 13, 2025 must not result in new task records being created.

events.input: Submitter: Sarah Chen (sarah.chen@company.com), Software Engineer. OOO activation: starting Monday June 9, 2025 through Friday June 13, 2025. Submitted at 2025-06-08T17:45:00Z.

expect.action: 1) Notification posted to the Engineering Team Slack channel announcing Sarah Chen's OOO absence (June 9–13, 2025). 2) Task-capture mode activated in task-tracker.
expect.reason: ooo-status-policy, upon receiving an OOO activation event, composes and sends an Engineering Team notification to engineering-team-slack and simultaneously sends an activation signal to task-tracker; engineering-team-slack posts the notification to the Engineering Team Slack channel, and task-tracker enables task-capture mode.

events.input: Reacji event: User @daniel_okafor reacted with 🔧 emoji to a message in #general from @lena_kim (message ID: msg-20250610-0931, message text: 'Hey team, the staging deployment pipeline is broken — can someone with infra access take a look at the Terraform config in PR #519?', message timestamp: 2025-06-10T09:31:00Z, reactor: @daniel_okafor, reaction timestamp: 2025-06-10T11:15:00Z, channel: #general).

expect.action: New task record inserted into the Zapier Tables database capturing: original message 'Hey team, the staging deployment pipeline is broken — can someone with infra access take a look at the Terraform config in PR #519?', requester @lena_kim, reactor @daniel_okafor, emoji 🔧, message timestamp 2025-06-10T09:31:00Z, reaction timestamp 2025-06-10T11:15:00Z, channel #general, status Pending.
expect.reason: While OOO mode is active, task-tracker creates a new task record for each Reacji event and writes it to task-store, which inserts it into the Zapier Tables database.

events.input: Reacji event: User @priya_patel reacted with 📋 emoji to a message in #general from @tom_nguyen (message ID: msg-20250613-1730, message text: 'Can someone update the onboarding docs to reflect the new SSO flow introduced in sprint 42?', message timestamp: 2025-06-13T17:30:00Z, reactor: @priya_patel, reaction timestamp: 2025-06-13T17:32:00Z, channel: #general).

expect.action: No task record created; no external output produced.
expect.reason: M001 specifies that task-capture mode is automatically disabled at 5:00 PM on Friday June 13, 2025, and the Reacji event arrives at 17:32 on June 13, 2025 — after the cutoff — so no new task record may be created.

events.input: Dashboard status update action submitted by Sarah Chen (sarah.chen@company.com) at 2025-06-16T09:10:00Z. Task ID: TASK-001 (original message: 'the staging deployment pipeline is broken — can someone with infra access take a look at the Terraform config in PR #519?', requester: @daniel_okafor). New status: 'In Progress'. Updated by: Sarah Chen upon return from OOO.

expect.action: 1) TASK-001 record updated to status 'In Progress' in the Zapier Tables database. 2) Slack message sent to Sarah Chen (the requester on record for TASK-001 per the reference data) notifying them that TASK-001 status has been updated to 'In Progress'.
expect.reason: When a status update arrives from the ooo-dashboard, task-tracker updates the task record in task-store and sends a status notification to the original requester via requester-notifier; task-store updates the matching record in the database, and requester-notifier sends a Slack message to the original requester (@daniel_okafor).

objects.role: Entry-point service object that detects emoji reactions on Slack messages and forwards them into the automation.
objects.state_description: Records incoming emoji reaction events including user, message, channel, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a 📝 emoji reaction event arrives on a Slack message, forward the event details (reactor user ID, message timestamp, channel ID) to the thread-summarizer peer.

peers.relationship: Forward emoji reaction event details (user ID, message timestamp, channel ID) when a 📝 reaction is detected.

event_sources: webhook: Slack emoji reaction added event

objects.role: Read service that retrieves the full content of a Slack thread given a channel and message timestamp.
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for thread content by fetching all messages in a Slack thread. When answering queries, call the `slack_threads_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that orchestrates thread retrieval, summary generation, and delivery of the summary to the user.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight summarization requests including requesting user, channel, message timestamp, retrieved thread content, and generated summary.
objects.behavior: When a 📝 reaction event arrives, query the slack-threads read service for the full thread content using the channel ID and message timestamp. When thread content is received, send it to the openai-summarizer for summary generation. When a summary is returned, send the summary and the requesting user ID to the slack-dm service for delivery.

peers.relationship: Query for full thread content using channel ID and message timestamp when a reaction event is received.
Send retrieved thread content for summarization once thread content is available.
Send the generated summary and requesting user ID for DM delivery once the summary is ready.

objects.role: Read service that generates a concise summary of a Slack thread using OpenAI.
objects.behavior: Responds to requests containing thread message content by generating a concise, readable summary. When answering queries, call the `openai_summarizer_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service that sends a direct message to a Slack user with the thread summary.
objects.state_description: Records all DMs sent including recipient user ID, summary content, and delivery timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a summary and user ID are received, send the summary as a direct message to the specified Slack user.

steps.text: User Maya Chen reacted with the 📝 emoji to a Slack message in channel #product-updates (channel ID: C04XYZABC). The reacted message has timestamp 1718000000.123456 and was posted by Alex Rivera. Maya Chen's user ID is U09MAYACHEN.

expect.action: A direct message is sent to Maya Chen (U09MAYACHEN) containing a concise summary of the Slack thread in #product-updates starting at message timestamp 1718000000.123456.
expect.reason: slack-events forwards the reaction event to thread-summarizer, which retrieves the thread via slack-threads, sends it to openai-summarizer for a summary, and then slack-dm delivers the summary to Maya Chen's user ID.

objects.role: Entry-point service representing the HR self-service portal that receives employee employment verification letter requests.
objects.state_description: Records of submitted verification letter requests including employee ID, requester details, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an employee submits an employment verification letter request through the portal, forward the request details to the verification-letter-policy object. Record the submission in state.

peers.relationship: Forward employee verification request details (employee ID, requester info, purpose) when a new request is submitted

event_sources: external system: employee submits employment verification letter request via HR self-service portal

objects.role: Read service representing Workday that retrieves employee data on demand.
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for employee data including name, job title, department, employment start date, employment status, and manager. When answering queries, call the `workday_directory_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies employment verification letter rules, orchestrates data retrieval, generates the letter, and routes it for approval.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight verification requests with their status (data-retrieved, letter-generated, pending-approval, approved, delivered), associated employee data, and generated document references.
objects.behavior: When a verification request arrives from hr-portal, query workday-directory for the employee's full name, job title, department, employment start date, employment status, and salary (if authorized). Once employee data is returned, use the generate-pdf-letter skill to produce a professionally formatted employment verification letter PDF. Then forward the generated letter and request context to bamboohr-approval for HR Manager review. When an approval decision arrives from bamboohr-approval: if approved, send the letter PDF and employee email address to email-delivery; if rejected, update state to rejected and notify hr-portal of the outcome.

peers.relationship: Query for employee data (name, title, department, start date, status) when a verification request is received
Send approved letter PDF and employee email address for delivery when approval is confirmed
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing BambooHR workflow that routes documents for HR Manager approval and returns approval decisions.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending and completed approval tasks with document reference, approver, decision, and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a letter and request context arrive from verification-letter-policy, create an approval task in BambooHR for the HR Manager and record it in state. When an HR Manager approval or rejection event arrives from BambooHR, forward the decision (approved or rejected) along with the document reference back to verification-letter-policy via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward HR Manager approval or rejection decision with document reference when a decision event is received

event_sources: external system: HR Manager submits approval or rejection decision in BambooHR workflow

objects.role: Write service that delivers the approved employment verification letter PDF to the employee via email.
objects.state_description: Log of delivered emails including recipient address, document reference, delivery timestamp, and status.
objects.behavior: When an approved letter PDF and employee email address are received, send the letter as an email attachment to the employee and record the delivery in state.

steps.text: Employee Marcus Thompson requests an employment verification letter through the HR self-service portal. Employee ID: MT-4821, email: marcus.thompson@company.com. The letter is for a standard employment verification (no salary disclosure requested).

expect.action: Employment verification letter delivered to marcus.thompson@company.com as a PDF attachment, confirming Marcus Thompson's employment details including job title, department, employment start date, and employment status, following HR Manager approval via BambooHR workflow.
expect.reason: hr-portal forwards the request to verification-letter-policy, which retrieves employee data from workday-directory, generates the PDF letter, routes it through bamboohr-approval for HR Manager sign-off, and upon approval email-delivery sends the letter to the employee's email address.

steps.text: HR Manager Jennifer Okafor approves the employment verification letter request for Marcus Thompson in the BambooHR workflow. Approval decision: Approved. Task ID: BHRAPP-20241.

expect.action: Approved employment verification letter PDF sent to marcus.thompson@company.com as an email attachment via email-delivery.
expect.reason: bamboohr-approval receives the HR Manager's approval decision and forwards the approved letter and employee email address to email-delivery, which sends the letter to the employee.

expect.action: The hr-portal records the submission. The terminal output is likely an error or failed lookup with no approval task created.
expect.reason: When a letter and request context arrive from verification-letter-policy, bamboohr-approval creates an approval task in BambooHR for the HR Manager and records it in state.

expect.reason: When bamboohr-approval forwards an approved decision to verification-letter-policy, verification-letter-policy sends the letter PDF and employee email address to email-delivery, which delivers the letter to the employee.
