objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the online HR portal form that receives employee verification letter requests.
objects.state_description: Records submitted verification letter requests including employee ID, request timestamp, and request details.
objects.behavior: When a verification letter request is submitted via the HR portal form, forward the request payload to the verification-letter-policy business logic object. Record the submission in state.

peers.relationship: Forward employee verification letter request details when a new form submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: employee submits verification letter request via HR portal form

objects.role: Read service representing Workday that retrieves employee details on demand.
objects.behavior: Responds to queries for employee details such as name, job title, department, employment start date, employment status, and manager. When answering queries, call the `workday_directory_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies verification letter rules, orchestrates data retrieval, and drives letter generation and delivery.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight verification letter requests including employee ID, retrieved employee details, generated document reference, and delivery status.
objects.behavior: When a verification letter request arrives from hr-portal, query workday-directory for the employee's details (name, job title, department, employment start date, employment status). Once employee details are retrieved, send them to google-docs-generator to produce the formatted verification letter. When the generated document reference is returned from google-docs-generator, send the document reference and employee email address to email-delivery to deliver the completed letter to the employee.

peers.relationship: Query for employee details when a new verification letter request is received
Send employee details and letter template instructions to generate the verification letter
Send the completed letter document reference and employee email address to deliver the letter
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Read/write service representing Google Docs that generates a professional verification letter from a customizable template and returns the document reference.
objects.state_description: Tracks generated documents with their document IDs, employee references, and generation timestamps.
objects.behavior: When employee details and template instructions are received, create a professional verification letter using the customizable Google Docs template populated with the provided employee data. Return the generated document reference (URL or ID) to the caller. When answering queries for generated document references, call the `google_docs_generator_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service representing the email notification system that delivers the completed verification letter to the employee.
objects.state_description: Records sent emails including recipient address, document reference, send timestamp, and delivery status.
objects.behavior: When a document reference and recipient email address are received, send the verification letter to the employee via email with the document attached or linked. Record the delivery in state.

steps.text: Employee Marcus Thompson, Senior Financial Analyst in the Finance department, submits a verification letter request via the HR portal form. Request includes: employee name Marcus Thompson, employee email marcus.thompson@company.com, purpose of letter: international travel visa application.

expect.action: Verification letter emailed to marcus.thompson@company.com containing Marcus Thompson's employment details (job title: Senior Financial Analyst, department: Finance, employment start date, employment status) generated from the Google Docs template, with the document attached or linked.
expect.reason: hr-portal forwards the request to verification-letter-policy, which queries workday-directory for employee details, instructs google-docs-generator to produce the letter, and triggers email-delivery to send the completed letter to marcus.thompson@company.com.

modifications.intent: For the next little while, hold off on sending out the letters until later in the day

events.input: Employee verification letter request submitted via HR portal form. Employee name: Priya Nair. Employee ID: EMP-4821. Employee email: priya.nair@company.com. Job title: Senior Software Engineer. Department: Engineering. Employment start date: March 15, 2019. Employment status: Full-time, Active. Purpose of letter: Business travel visa application for upcoming client engagement in Germany. Request timestamp: W03-1T14:30. Request ID: REQ-20231.

expect.action: No verification letter email is sent; the workday-directory query for employee EMP-4821 (Priya Nair) returns no matching record, so verification-letter-policy cannot proceed with letter generation or delivery.
expect.reason: Under baseline behavior, verification-letter-policy orchestrates retrieval, letter generation, and delivery; email-delivery sends the completed letter to the employee's email address upon receiving the document reference and recipient email.

events.input: Employee verification letter request submitted via HR portal form. Employee name: Daniel Osei. Employee ID: EMP-3374. Employee email: daniel.osei@company.com. Job title: Marketing Manager. Department: Marketing. Employment start date: July 8, 2020. Employment status: Full-time, Active. Purpose of letter: International travel visa application for conference attendance in Japan. Request timestamp: W03-2T10:15. Request ID: REQ-20244.

expect.action: Verification letter request for Daniel Osei (REQ-20244) is held; no letter is sent at this time.
expect.reason: M001 is active at W03-2T10:15, instructing verification-letter-policy to hold off on sending out letters until later in the day, so the terminal output of delivery is deferred and no email is sent.

objects.role: Entry-point service object representing the Zapier Interfaces form where the project manager submits weekly project updates.
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted form payloads including project name, update content, submission timestamp, and submitting PM identity.
objects.behavior: When a project manager submits a weekly update via the form, forward the full update payload to the stakeholder-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full project update payload when a new form submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: form submitted via Zapier Interfaces

objects.role: Write service representing Zapier Tables where submitted project updates are stored for status tracking.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all submitted project updates including project name, update content, PM identity, submission timestamp, approval status, and delivery status.
objects.behavior: When a project update record is received, store it with its current status. When an approval or delivery status change is received, update the corresponding record's status field.

objects.role: Read service representing the Notion database storing the project charter and stakeholder role mappings.
objects.state_description: No local state — retrieves project charter details and stakeholder role mappings on demand.
objects.behavior: Answers queries about project charters and stakeholder role mappings by project. When answering queries, call the `notion_charter_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that identifies appropriate stakeholders for a given project update and orchestrates the drafting and approval workflow.
objects.state_description: Tracks in-flight update requests including project update content, identified stakeholders and their roles, drafted email content, and approval state per update.
objects.behavior: When a project update payload arrives, store it to update-store and query notion-charter for the project charter and stakeholder role mappings to identify the appropriate stakeholders. Once stakeholder roles are resolved, draft a tailored status update email customized to each stakeholder role and the project context. Send the draft email to slack-approval for PM review. When an approval confirmation arrives from slack-approval, send the finalized email to gmail-delivery addressed to the identified stakeholders. When a rejection or revision request arrives from slack-approval, revise the draft and re-send to slack-approval.

peers.relationship: Send project update record for storage immediately upon receiving a new submission, and send status updates when approval or delivery state changes
Query for project charter and stakeholder role mappings when a new update arrives
Send approved email content and recipient list once PM approval is confirmed
Receives payload as described in this object's behavior (auto-added by derive_clean_samples.py to repair peer-behavior mismatch).

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing Slack, used to send draft emails to the PM for approval and to receive the PM's approval or rejection response.
objects.state_description: Tracks pending approval requests sent to the PM including draft content, stakeholder list, and approval state.
objects.behavior: When a draft email and stakeholder list are received from stakeholder-routing, post them to the PM's Slack channel requesting approval or revision. When the PM responds with an approval or rejection action via Slack, forward the response to stakeholder-routing via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward PM approval or rejection response when the PM acts on a draft email review message

event_sources: webhook: Slack interactive action event (PM approval or rejection response)

objects.role: Write service object representing Gmail, used to deliver approved stakeholder update emails to identified recipients.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all emails sent including recipient list, subject, body, project reference, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When an approved email with recipient list is received, send the email via Gmail to all identified stakeholders and record the delivery in state.

steps.text: Project manager Sarah Chen submits a weekly project update via the Zapier Interfaces form. Project: 'Enterprise CRM Migration'. Update details: Phase 2 backend integration is 75% complete, on track for the August 15 milestone. Key risks: third-party API rate limits may slow data sync. Blockers: none currently. Next steps: finalize data mapping with vendor by end of week.

expect.action: Draft stakeholder update email prepared for 'Enterprise CRM Migration' and posted to Sarah Chen's Slack channel requesting approval, along with the identified stakeholder recipient list derived from the Notion project charter.
expect.reason: project-form forwards the update payload to stakeholder-routing, which stores the record in update-store, queries notion-charter for stakeholder role mappings, drafts a tailored email, and routes it to slack-approval to request PM approval.

steps.text: Project manager Sarah Chen responds via Slack interactive action approving the draft stakeholder update email for 'Enterprise CRM Migration'. Slack channel: #pm-approvals, thread_ts: 1712345678.000100. Response: 'Approved — looks good, please send.'

expect.action: Approved stakeholder update email for 'Enterprise CRM Migration' delivered via Gmail to all identified stakeholders, and the corresponding record in Zapier Tables updated to 'delivered' status.
expect.reason: slack-approval receives the PM's approval response and forwards the approved email and recipient list to stakeholder-routing, which triggers gmail-delivery to send the email and update-store to update the record status.

modifications.intent: When drafting a stakeholder update email, if the project update content mentions a milestone date that falls within 14 calendar days of the submission date, prepend the email body with the text 'MILESTONE ALERT: A project milestone is approaching within 14 days. Immediate attention may be required.' on its own line before the standard update content. If no milestone date within 14 days is mentioned, draft the email as normal without the banner.

events.input: Project manager David Nguyen submits a weekly project update via the Zapier Interfaces form. Project: 'Cloud Infrastructure Overhaul'. Update details: Phase 3 network segmentation is 60% complete, currently on track for the September 30 milestone. Key risks: hardware procurement delays from vendor could push timeline by one week. Blockers: none at this time. Next steps: complete firewall rule configuration and schedule load testing with the DevOps team by end of month. Submission timestamp: W02-1T11:15. Submitting PM: David Nguyen.

expect.action: 1) A new project update record for 'Cloud Infrastructure Overhaul' is stored in Zapier Tables. 2) A draft stakeholder update email for 'Cloud Infrastructure Overhaul' is posted to the PM's Slack channel (#pm-approvals) requesting approval or revision from the PM.
expect.reason: project-form forwards the submitted update to stakeholder-routing, which stores it in update-store and queries notion-charter for stakeholder info, drafts a tailored email, and sends it to slack-approval, which posts it to the PM's Slack channel for review.

events.input: Project manager Sarah Chen submits a weekly project update via the Zapier Interfaces form. Project: 'Enterprise CRM Migration'. Update details: Phase 2 backend integration is 90% complete. The go-live milestone is scheduled for W02-9 (9 days from today), marking the completion of the full data migration. Key risks: final UAT sign-off from the client is pending and must be received by W02-7 to avoid delay. Blockers: none currently. Next steps: coordinate UAT session with client stakeholders and confirm deployment window with infrastructure team. Submission timestamp: W02-3T10:00. Submitting PM: Sarah Chen.

expect.action: 1) A new project update record for 'Enterprise CRM Migration' is stored in Zapier Tables. 2) A draft stakeholder update email for 'Enterprise CRM Migration' — with the body prepended with 'MILESTONE ALERT: A project milestone is approaching within 14 days. Immediate attention may be required.' on its own line — is posted to the PM's Slack channel (#pm-approvals) requesting approval or revision from Sarah Chen.
expect.reason: M001 is active and the submitted update references a go-live milestone on W02-9 (9 days from submission date W02-3), which falls within 14 calendar days, so the milestone alert banner is prepended to the draft email before it is posted to #pm-approvals for PM review.

events.input: Project manager Sarah Chen responds via Slack interactive action approving the draft stakeholder update email for 'Enterprise CRM Migration'. Slack channel: #pm-approvals, thread_ts: 1712345999.000200. Response: 'Approved — the milestone alert banner is appropriate, please send to all stakeholders.' Approving PM: Sarah Chen.

expect.action: Approved stakeholder update email for 'Enterprise CRM Migration' (with the milestone alert banner) is sent via Gmail to executive-sponsors@acmecorp.com, it-leadership@acmecorp.com, and crm-migration-stakeholders@acmecorp.com, and the delivery is recorded.
expect.reason: slack-approval forwards Sarah Chen's approval response to stakeholder-routing, which then sends the finalized email to gmail-delivery addressed to the identified stakeholders, and gmail-delivery sends the email and records the delivery.

events.input: Project manager James Okafor submits a weekly project update via the Zapier Interfaces form. Project: 'HR Portal Redesign'. Update details: UI component library integration is 45% complete, on track for the Q4 delivery. Key risks: design review feedback from the UX team is still pending for two screens. Blockers: none. Next steps: complete accessibility audit on the navigation module and hand off to QA team for regression testing. Submission timestamp: W02-3T14:30. Submitting PM: James Okafor.

expect.action: stakeholder-routing receives the project update payload from project-form via peer message. stakeholder-routing then calls update-store_write to store the update, and calls notion_charter_data to query for the 'HR Portal Redesign' project charter and stakeholder role mappings. The notion_charter_data tool returns only data for 'PROJ-4471 Enterprise CRM Migration' — there is no entry for 'HR Portal Redesign'. With no stakeholder role mappings or distribution list returned, stakeholder-routing cannot identify stakeholders or draft a tailored email, so the workflow terminates after the store and query steps with no draft sent to slack-approval.
expect.reason: The stakeholder-routing behavior requires querying notion-charter to resolve stakeholder roles before drafting and routing emails; since the reference data contains no charter or stakeholder mappings for 'HR Portal Redesign', the query returns no usable data and the workflow cannot proceed past the lookup step, producing no further terminal outputs beyond the update-store write and the notion-charter query.
